dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & verification

**Legal entity (VERIFIED -- TX Comptroller).** The registered entity is **CONQUER ELECTRICAL LLC** (note: the prospecting seed said "Conquer Electrical Services LLC" -- the Comptroller record shows the legal name is "Conquer Electrical LLC," with "Conquer Electrical Services" used as the registered-agent/dba name). TX Taxpayer Number **32079256544 -> 32079529882**, Texas SOS File Number **0804094479**, State of Formation **TX**, **Effective SOS Registration Date 06/04/2021** (so ~5 years old, not a brand-new startup). (VERIFIED)

**Owner (VERIFIED -- Comptroller 2023 Public Information Report).** Director and Owner: **YOVANI TAMACAS**, 15402 Old Creek (Rd), Houston, TX 77060. This confirms and completes the seed's "Yovani." (VERIFIED)

**Vertical.** Residential electrical: installs/repairs of wiring, fixtures, panels, EV chargers, ceiling fans, lighting. (VERIFIED via Yelp/Yahoo listings)

**Service area (CORRECTION to seed).** The seed claimed "Greater Houston incl. west side." The mailing/registered address is **15402 Old Creek Rd, Houston, TX 77060** -- that is **North Houston / Greenspoint**, NOT the west side. BuildZoom permit history shows past jobs in North Houston and Kingwood (NE), reinforcing a north/northeast footprint rather than Memorial/Energy Corridor west. Any "serves Greater Houston" claim is marketing, not a west-side base. (VERIFIED address; service-radius claim UNVERIFIED)

**Entity / right-to-operate status (VERIFIED -- major flag).** The Comptroller record states **Right to Transact Business in Texas: FRANCHISE TAX INVOLUNTARILY ENDED** and **SOS Registration Status: INACTIVE**. The LLC has effectively lost its good standing for franchise-tax reasons. (VERIFIED)

**License status (VERIFIED lapsed on two fronts).** Texas residential electrical contracting requires a current TDLR license.
- Company **TECL / Electrician #36233** -- BuildZoom shows expiration **07/20/2023**; the **live TDLR active-license search (active licenses only) returns "No Records Found"** for #36233, confirming it is not active. (VERIFIED lapsed)
- Owner's individual license **RW (Residential Wireman) #231993, Harris County** -- live TDLR name search returns it tagged **"Expired" (exp 03/04/2026)**; BuildZoom labels it Inactive/cancelled. A Residential Wireman is the entry-level individual classification that must work under a licensed master/contractor and cannot independently pull permits or hold a company contractor license. (VERIFIED expired)

**Active confirmation.** Phone (832) 370-5332 is published on Yelp/Yahoo (BuildZoom also lists 281-901-7963 and 832-785-4386). **Website conquerelectrical.com did NOT load on any attempt this session** (3 WebFetch timeouts, a ddg-fetch timeout, a frozen browser renderer, and multiple failed browser navigations) -- it is effectively unreachable. The business still appears to take inquiries via Yelp (2-min response time advertised), but its own web presence is down. (Site-down VERIFIED this session; whether permanent is UNVERIFIED.)

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

**Yelp.** 5.0 stars (VERIFIED rating). **Review COUNT is uncertain and must not be asserted:** the Yelp page title consistently reads "32 Photos & **12 Reviews**," yet aggregator snapshots this session reported **28, 39, and 40** reviews at different times. Treat the count as **unverified (likely ~12-40)** pending a live check. Yelp also advertises "Response 2 mins / 100% response rate." (rating VERIFIED; count UNVERIFIED)

**Google.** No Google Business Profile surfaced in any search -- Google review count/rating unknown and possibly unclaimed/weak. (UNVERIFIED / not found)

**Facebook.** A page (facebook.com/conquerelectricalservices) exists; one aggregated snippet reported ~87 likes -- very small. (UNVERIFIED)

**Other platforms.** No Angi, HomeAdvisor, Thumbtack, Nextdoor, or BBB profile found for this specific company. (The "Conquest Electrical Contracting" of Austin and "Conquer Services LLC" of Odessa are different businesses -- do not conflate.)

**Review themes (proof the service is good).** Recurring praise: extremely fast response (replies in ~5 minutes, same-day/next-morning scheduling), fair/competitive pricing, quick diagnosis-and-fix (one job "quoted and fixed within 20 minutes"), same-day EV-charger and fixture installs, and Yovani described as "the most professional and organized electrician" who responded. **This is genuine demand proof: the operator converts fast and customers love him.** No recurring complaints surfaced (consistent 5.0). The pain point is therefore NOT service quality -- it is that this quality is invisible and uncapturable because the funnel is broken.

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

**Website.** conquerelectrical.com is referenced by directory listings (Yelp/Yahoo) but **does not serve a functional page** -- it timed out/failed to render on every tool and browser attempt this session. A dead homepage means 100% of paid or organic clicks that land on the site bounce with zero capture. (Platform/builder could not be identified because the page never loaded.) (VERIFIED unreachable this session)

**Booking.** No functioning online booking on their own property. The only working intake mechanisms are: (a) the **Yelp "Request a Quote" button** -- which is Yelp-owned, puts Yelp between them and the customer, and pushes Yelp Ads upsells; and (b) **phone** (832) 370-5332. This is effectively **manual text/phone quoting**, the exact friction Royal Pawz had before automated booking. (VERIFIED)

**Payment.** No online payment found; almost certainly invoice/cash/Zelle at the job. (UNVERIFIED, inferred)

**Follow-up automation.** None detected -- no SMS/email nurture, no missed-call text-back. Lead "capture" depends on Yovani personally answering Yelp within 2 minutes; any lead that comes in while he's on a ladder is lost. (UNVERIFIED negative)

**SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** **The business has no working owned funnel.** Its website is down and its only lead path is a Yelp-owned quote button. Every dollar of search intent for "electrician near me" either never reaches them or dies on a broken homepage. Even a basic landing-page-plus-online-booking would be a step-change -- but see the fit caveat in Section 8.

## 4. Acquisition & ad presence

**Google Ads:** none detected. No ad copy, no Google Ads Transparency presence found. (UNVERIFIED negative)
**Meta ads:** no Meta Ad Library results found for "Conquer Electrical" (the live fetch failed, so this is not 100% confirmed, but no ad evidence appeared anywhere). (UNVERIFIED negative)
**Platform dependency:** effectively **100% dependent on Yelp** for inbound, plus word-of-mouth. No Angi/Thumbtack/HomeAdvisor footprint. (VERIFIED Yelp-centric)
**Pixels:** could not assess -- site never loaded. (Unknown)
**SEO footprint:** essentially invisible. No Google Business Profile surfaced; the brand only ranks for its own exact name (Yelp, BuildZoom, Yahoo). It does **not** rank for competitive terms like "Houston electrician" or "electrician near me" -- dominant incumbents own those. (VERIFIED weak)

## 5. Owner / decision-maker

**Yovani Tamacas** -- Director/Owner (VERIFIED, Comptroller PIR). Operates as a solo/owner-operator electrician; the brand IS him (every positive review names "Yovani"). Likely **bilingual / Hispanic-owned, family small business** (surname Tamacas; two other Tamacas individuals -- Henry, Jose -- appear in TDLR with electrical licenses, suggesting a family trade). Personality signals: fast, responsive, customer-friendly, hands-on -- the kind of operator who replies in minutes and fixes problems on the spot. Direct contact: (832) 370-5332 (published). No LinkedIn or personal-brand presence found. (Owner VERIFIED; bilingual/family angle UNVERIFIED inference.)

## 6. Competitive landscape

West Houston electrical is dominated by SEO-and-review heavyweights this prospect cannot currently touch:
- **West Houston Electric (Katy/Richmond)** -- BBB-accredited, 30+ years, **~1,800+ five-star reviews** (Birdeye aggregates 2,600+), 4.8 rating, 24/7 answering, full service menu and online presence. (VERIFIED)
- **Mister Sparky / national franchises and "Texas Electrical Residential Contractors"** and similar -- heavy Google Ads, booking widgets, and review volume in the hundreds.

What they have that Conquer lacks: a working website, online booking, claimed Google Business Profile, hundreds-to-thousands of reviews, paid search, and -- critically -- **current, valid licensure and good standing**. Where Conquer could realistically win in a normal scenario: hyper-fast response and price on small residential jobs (panels, EV chargers, fixtures) in its north Houston base. **But that "win" is blocked by the compliance problems** (lapsed licenses, forfeited LLC), which a competitor or an informed homeowner can expose in seconds.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

**Ticket size.** Residential electrical service calls in Houston typically run **$150-$600** for small repairs/diagnostics, **$1,200-$3,000+** for panel upgrades, and **$500-$1,500** for EV-charger installs. Blended ~**$350-$600** average ticket for a small-job-heavy solo operator. (Industry-typical, UNVERIFIED for this firm.)
**Paid-search economics (West/North Houston).** "Electrician" is a high-CPC category ($8-$25+/click); a tuned campaign + landing page commonly yields **~$120-$300 CAC** and **3x-6x ROAS** once booking friction is removed. Seasonality: storm season (spring/early summer) and holiday-lighting/heating-load season (Nov-Jan) spike demand; summer AC-circuit and generator interest is strong in Houston.
**Royal-Pawz-style lift math.** If a working landing page + online booking lifted booked-job conversion the way Royal Pawz went 7% -> 30.8% (~4x), and the operator currently converts, say, 20 inbound inquiries/month at a ~$450 ticket: moving from ~30% to ~60% close on a fixed lead flow would add ~6 jobs/month x $450 = **~$2,700/mo (~$32k/yr)** -- before adding paid traffic. With paid search layered on top at 4x ROAS, the upside is larger. **The mechanics would clearly pay for a Zapp engagement IF the business were eligible to receive the work.**

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

**Why-them-why-now (the lead gap):** The operator demonstrably converts customers fast and earns 5-star reviews, but his **owned funnel is non-existent** -- the website won't load and his only lead path is a Yelp-owned quote button. That is a textbook Zapp problem (broken intake on top of real service quality).

**BUT -- this is a no-go on diligence, and that controls the recommendation.** Three independent primary-source red flags stack up:
1. **Company electrical contractor license (TECL #36233) is lapsed** (expired 07/20/2023; absent from active TDLR search).
2. **Owner's individual license (RW #231993) is expired** (and is only a Residential Wireman tier, which cannot hold a contractor license anyway).
3. **The LLC's right to transact business is "FRANCHISE TAX INVOLUNTARILY ENDED" and SOS status is INACTIVE.**

Zapp's model drives MORE customers to a business. Pouring paid demand into an operator who is **not currently licensed to legally contract electrical work and whose LLC is in tax forfeiture** creates real liability and brand risk for both the client and Zapp, and any spend could be wasted if he's forced to pause operations. The right call is **Not a fit until cured.** If, on a live check, Yovani has reinstated the TECL (or partnered under a master) AND reinstated the LLC, this flips to a strong **Revenue System Build** candidate (great reviews + zero funnel = ideal Zapp setup). Until then, do not sell.

**Recommended tier:** Not a fit (re-evaluate as **Revenue System Build** only after licensure + entity good standing are restored and verified).
**Fit score:** 24/100 -- the service quality and funnel gap would otherwise score ~70, but the trifecta of lapsed contractor license, expired owner license, and forfeited LLC drags it down hard.
**Diligence flags:** lapsed TECL #36233; expired RW #231993 (and tier too low to contract); LLC franchise-tax forfeiture / SOS inactive; website down; name discrepancy (legal name "Conquer Electrical LLC" vs seed "Conquer Electrical Services LLC"); address is North Houston 77060, not the west side claimed in the seed.

## 9. Sources

6. TX Comptroller Franchise Tax Account Status -- CONQUER ELECTRICAL LLC, taxpayer 32079529882: https://comptroller.texas.gov/taxes/franchise/account-status/search/32079529882 (formed 06/04/2021; SOS file 0804094479; Right to Transact = FRANCHISE TAX INVOLUNTARILY ENDED; SOS INACTIVE; Director/Owner YOVANI TAMACAS per 2023 PIR)
7. TDLR live active-license search: https://www.tdlr.texas.gov/LicenseSearch/ (#36233 = "No Records Found"; "Conquer Electrical" name = no records; "Tamacas" name = RW-231993 "Expired," exp 03/04/2026 -- confirms both licenses lapsed)

West Houston Electric (competitor) -- Yelp: https://www.yelp.com/biz/west-houston-electric-katy-2 and site https://www.westhoustonelectric.com/electrical-services/ and Angi: https://www.angi.com/companylist/us/tx/katy/west-houston-electric-inc-reviews-2695247.htm (1,800+ five-star reviews, BBB-accredited incumbent)

verification.notes:
Biggest correction: the prior analyst WRONGLY concluded there is no Google Business Profile. There IS one and it is the single most pitch-relevant asset: 'Conquer Electrical Services', 5.0 stars / 19 Google reviews, active (reviews within ~4 months), Open 24 hours, Latino-owned. Critically, the GBP has NO website linked (shows 'Add website') — so the only digital storefront is the GBP itself plus Yelp; the owned domain conquerelectrical.com is effectively dead (re-confirmed unreachable on 2026-06-07). A pitch should center on: (1) a real review base (19 Google + Yelp 5.0/12) that is being wasted because there is no working website or booking funnel; (2) the conversion gap — strong reputation but phone-only intake, no online booking/payment, no site. 

COMPLIANCE LANDMINES the pitch must treat carefully and NOT overstate: (a) Owner's individual Residential Wireman license RW-231993 EXPIRED 03/04/2026 — only ~3 months before today (2026-06-07), i.e. a RECENT lapse, not long-dead; frame as 'currently shows expired, likely a renewal-lapse worth confirming with them' rather than 'never licensed'. (b) Company TECL #36233 lapsed (BuildZoom: expired 07-20-2023). (c) The LLC's Right to Transact Business = FRANCHISE TAX INVOLUNTARILY ENDED and SOS status INACTIVE — the entity itself is not in good standing. These are real flags; do not soft-pedal but also note they are administrative/renewal issues, recoverable via reinstatement, and the business is clearly still operating (24/7 hours, reviews 4 months ago). 

Data-source caveats: BuildZoom is STALE/unreliable on this entity (it listed the owner's RW license as 'expired 2014' and named an unrelated 'Jon Philippe Laborde' as master — both contradicted by authoritative TDLR/Comptroller live data; trust TDLR + Comptroller, not BuildZoom, for license/owner facts). Yelp review COUNT is genuinely noisy (page title and multiple snapshots show 12; aggregators showed 28/39/40) — cite Google's 19 as the cleaner, freshly-verified number and treat Yelp count as approximate. Meta 'ads' for 'Conquer Electrical' are pure keyword noise (unrelated advertisers); do not claim they advertise. No BBB/Angi/Nextdoor/Thumbtack profile found.

dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & verification

**Name correction (VERIFIED):** The seed name "His Kingdom Electrical LLC" is slightly wrong. The legal entity is **HIS KINGDOM ELECTRIC LLC** ("Electric," not "Electrical"). The seed service-area note ("Houston / Katy") is also imprecise — the business is registered and based in **Waller, TX 77484**, NW of Katy on the Harris/Waller county line. It markets to the greater NW Houston / Katy / Cypress / Waller / Brookshire corridor.

- **Legal entity (VERIFIED, TX SOS-mirrored record):** HIS KINGDOM ELECTRIC LLC — Texas Domestic LLC. Texas Tax ID **32095266055**; SOS File Number **0805566291**. SOS Charter Date & Responsibility Beginning Date **2024-05-28**. **Status: Active** (SOS status "Active," status date 2024-05-28). County of formation: **Harris**. Registered office: **300 W Tranquil Fields Ln, Waller, TX 77484**. (Source: texas-biz.com SOS mirror; corroborated by Bizapedia listing existing for the same entity. Comptroller's own franchise-status tool could not be queried headlessly this session — recommend a live confirm, but two independent SOS mirrors agree.)
- **Age (VERIFIED):** Formed May 28, 2024 — roughly **2 years old** as of June 2026. Genuinely an early-stage owner-operator, consistent with the seed thesis.
- **License (VERIFIED — and this is the headline diligence flag):** I downloaded the full TDLR **Electrical Contractor** license file (Lteecele.csv, ~13,900 active records, all of Texas) and found **NO Electrical Contractor license for "His Kingdom Electric"** under that name, and **no license tied to the 300 W Tranquil Fields Ln / Waller 77484 address**. The only "Kingdom" electrical contractors in the state file are unrelated companies in San Antonio, Lubbock, Abilene, Temple, Sanger and McKinney. I also searched the TDLR **Master Electrician** file (~19,700 records) and **Journeyman** file (~44,500 records) — no "His Kingdom" business linkage and no Tranquil Fields address. **In Texas, a business performing electrical work must hold a TDLR Electrical Contractor license backed by a designated master electrician.** I could not confirm one exists. (UNVERIFIED whether the owner personally holds a master/journeyman license under his own name that simply hasn't been attached to a contractor license — see Section 5.)
- **Active confirmation (VERIFIED):** Business is live and operating in 2026. The Facebook page shows a post dated **April 13** ("Another panel changeout successfully completed"), 113 followers, and a working phone/email. Phone **(832) 663-2512** and email **Hiskingdom89@gmail.com** are listed and appear valid. This corrects the draft note's implied "went quiet" read — activity is sparse but ongoing.
- **No standalone website found (VERIFIED):** No company domain surfaced across any search angle. Confirms the seed's "no standalone website" claim.

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

This is a near-zero-review-footprint business — strong signal of early stage, weak signal of proven demand at scale.

- **Google Business Profile (UNVERIFIED / likely none):** No Google Maps / GBP listing surfaced in any search. Appears **unclaimed or non-existent** — a major missed channel for a home-services electrician. (Flag for live confirm.)
- **Facebook (VERIFIED):** 113 followers, 1 following. **"Not yet rated (1 Review)."** Intro: "We specialize in all your Electrical needs. Panel Change outs, Generator installation, and more." Page category: Electrician. Followers grew from ~28 (when first indexed) to 113 — modest organic growth.
- **Nextdoor (VERIFIED):** "Hiskingdom Electric LLC," Waller TX (East Tranquil Fields). Three activity posts, all dated **Oct 20, 2024** — outdoor lighting, customized installs, outdoor patio wiring (lighting, ceiling fan, chandelier, receptacles). 1-2 reactions each. No visible review/recommend count.

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

This is a textbook thin-infrastructure owner-operator — the funnel barely exists.

- **Website:** **None found.** No domain, no landing page, no platform (no WordPress/Wix/UENI/Squarespace/GoDaddy artifact because there is no site at all). The "web presence" is a Facebook page + a Nextdoor page + a directory entry on Angi.
- **Mobile experience:** N/A — the only mobile touchpoint is the Facebook page, which is fine on mobile but is not a conversion surface.
- **Booking (VERIFIED):** **Phone-and-manual-quote only.** Every channel says the same thing: "Call us for your free quote." No online booking, no scheduling widget, no "Book Now" button, no web form. Lead path = call (832) 663-2512 or email Hiskingdom89@gmail.com, then a manual back-and-forth to schedule.
- **Payment:** **Unknown / not stated** anywhere public. No online-payment indicator. For a business this stage, near-certainly invoice/cash/Zelle/Venmo on completion (UNVERIFIED).
- **SMS/email follow-up automation:** **None detectable.** No evidence of any automated follow-up; lead handling is entirely manual via a personal Gmail (Hiskingdom89@gmail.com) and a cell number.
- **Lead-capture quality:** Poor. A prospect who finds them on Facebook or Nextdoor at 9pm has no way to book, see pricing, or leave details — they must remember to call. There is no capture of the lead's info, so every un-returned call is a permanently lost job.

**SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** There is **no owned conversion surface and no instant-response path** — no website, no online booking, no GBP, no SMS auto-reply. 100% of demand funnels through a single cell phone and a personal Gmail. Missed calls and after-hours interest evaporate with zero recapture. For a 2-year-old electrician with good reviews, this is the exact gap Zapp closes.

## 4. Acquisition & ad presence

- **Google Ads (VERIFIED — none):** No website to point ads to and no paid-search footprint surfaced. Not running Google Ads.

## 6. Competitive landscape

NW Houston / Katy electrical is a crowded, SEO-mature vertical. Dominant incumbents this prospect is invisible against:

- **Mister Sparky / Mr. Electric (franchise networks):** Heavy Google Ads spend, full booking sites, hundreds of Google reviews, branded fleet. Own the paid Map Pack.

**What they have that His Kingdom lacks:** a real website, claimed Google Business Profile with review volume, online booking/forms, paid-search presence, and (critically) a verifiable TDLR contractor license. **Where His Kingdom can realistically win:** the underserved hyper-local **Waller / Hockley / NW Cypress** fringe where the big franchises charge premium trip fees and respond slowly. ## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

- **Ticket size (typical residential electrical, West Houston):** Service calls/repairs commonly **$150–$600**; **panel changeouts $1,800–$4,000+**; **generator/standby installs $5,000–$12,000+**; EV chargers, outdoor lighting, whole-home rewires similar mid-four-figure range. His Kingdom's posted specialties (panel changeouts, generator installs, outdoor lighting/chandeliers) skew toward **high-ticket $1.5k–$10k jobs** — strong fit for a paid-acquisition system because one closed lead pays for a lot of ad spend.
- **Paid-search CAC / ROAS (electrical, West Houston — industry benchmark, UNVERIFIED for this exact prospect):** Electrical Google Ads CPCs run roughly **$8–$25/click**; typical cost-per-lead **$45–$120**; with a competent landing page + booking, **cost-per-booked-job often $150–$400**. Against a $2,500 average panel/job, that's a **6x–15x revenue-to-ad-spend** envelope — very healthy, which is why this vertical rewards the Zapp model.
- **Seasonality:** Steady year-round; surges around **summer (AC/electrical load, generator demand pre/post hurricane season — a Houston-specific spike)** and **winter storm preparedness** (post-2021 Texas grid anxiety keeps generator demand structurally high in this market).
- **Royal-Pawz-style lift, in HIS dollars (illustrative math):** Suppose he currently fields ~**20 inbound calls/leads a month** (Angi + Facebook + word of mouth) and converts ~**30%** to booked jobs = **6 jobs**. A typical mix averaging a conservative **$1,200/job** = **~$7,200/mo**. Royal Pawz roughly **4x'd** booking conversion (7% → 30.8%). Even a far-more-modest **doubling of captured/booked leads** here — via a website + online booking + instant SMS follow-up that stops the missed-call leakage and adds Google Ads + a claimed GBP feeding new leads — plausibly takes him from **6 → 12 booked jobs/mo**, i.e. **+$7,200/mo ≈ +$86k/yr** in recovered/new revenue, before counting the higher-ticket generator/panel jobs that a credible web presence makes him eligible to even bid. The leak today is so large (zero owned funnel) that the upside is dominated simply by capturing demand he's already generating and currently losing.

- **Why-them-why-now (one line):** A 2-year-old, faith-/owner-operated electrician with genuinely good word-of-mouth and high-ticket specialties (panels, generators) is funneling **100% of its demand through one cell phone and a Gmail** — no website, no Google Business Profile, no online booking, no follow-up — so every missed call and after-hours lead is lost. - **Fit score rationale (62):** Strong on the qualitative fit — true single-decision-maker owner-operator, thin/absent funnel (maximum build surface), high-ticket recurring-ish work, reachable by phone/email, likable faith-forward brand with real reviews. Docked meaningfully for: **(1) unresolved/likely-missing TDLR Electrical Contractor license** — a real compliance risk you must clear before running ads that drive regulated electrical work; **(2) very small scale / possibly thin cash to fund a Build now**; **(3) base address sits in Waller, not the affluent Memorial/Energy Corridor core**, so the premium-geo ROI framing requires him to be willing to serve and advertise into Katy/Cypress/Energy Corridor zips. He passes the early-stage screen, but the license flag keeps this out of "slam-dunk" territory.
- **Diligence flags:** (1) Confirm a TDLR Electrical Contractor license + designated master electrician exist before any ad spend. (2) Confirm owner identity/surname (do not assume "Edwin Larin"). (3) Confirm he can fund and operate at the lead volume a Build would generate.

- **Lead-capture quality:** decent fields, but every web lead is gated behind a human callback — after-hours, weekend, and high-intent "book now" demand stalls until someone replies.
- **SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** **The "booking" experience is a callback form, not instant self-scheduling.** A 4.8-star brand with strong organic visibility converts a fraction of its traffic because it forces a phone-tag step. In an emergency category (no-AC-in-Houston-summer), the operator who lets the customer lock a slot in 60 seconds wins the job. Closing this is the exact Royal-Pawz lever (frictionless online booking, 7% -> 30.8%).

- **Pixels:** Google Reviews widget present; Meta/Google pixel presence not confirmed (needs live network/source check).

The gap isn't traffic; it's **conversion of earned traffic** and **absence of a paid-search engine** to scale beyond organic + Thumbtack.

## 5. Owner / Decision-Maker
- **Owner:** Walter Jeffrey Washington Jr. (VERIFIED via TDLR licensee record). Sole owner/operator, hands-on technician — reviews repeatedly name "Walter."
- **Role:** Owner + lead tech + ACR-licensed contractor of record. ~2 employees (Thumbtack) — a true owner-operator.

Markets an HVAC apprenticeship program and branded merch — a builder/educator mindset, pride in the trade. **Family-owned** (stated on site). Likely receptive to a partner who amplifies the reputation he already earned rather than reinventing the brand.

## 6. Competitive Landscape (HVAC, West Houston / Katy)
- **BVS Home Experts** — family-owned since 1981, 24/7/365 emergency, deep review volume, established booking/dispatch infrastructure. Out-resources Washington on scale and emergency capacity.
- **Aire Serv of Katy** (Neighborly franchise) — national brand muscle, online scheduling, financing, polished UX and paid search.
- **Modern Air of Katy / Elmer's Home Services / Airtron** — strong SEO, online booking, review depth, and active ad spend.

- **Where Washington can win:** authentic owner-operator trust and "honest, no-upsell" positioning (a real differentiator against franchise upsell reputation), strong 4.8/~140-review social proof, and Class-A licensing. With frictionless booking + a tight paid-search funnel feeding those assets, Washington converts far better per-click and captures emergency demand it currently loses to the callback delay.

## 7. Vertical Economics & ROI Model
- **Ticket sizes (West Houston):** service/diagnostic $65-$150; common repairs $300-$900; capacitor/compressor/coil $500-$2,500; **full system replacement $7,000-$15,000+** — the high-ticket prize, especially in Houston's cooling season. Memorial / Briar Forest / Energy Corridor homes skew to premium, higher-SEER replacements and maintenance memberships — the affluent residential pocket to target (NOT the lower-income Westchase/Royal Oaks tracts).
- **Paid-search economics:** HVAC is among the most competitive local categories; "AC repair Katy" clicks ~$12-$30+, CAC per booked job commonly ~$80-$250 depending on conversion. With a $65 diagnostic converting into $300-$2,500 repairs (and occasional 5-figure replacements), even mid-tier ROAS is strongly profitable — IF the landing-to-booking step doesn't leak.
- **Seasonality:** demand spikes hard May-September (cooling), winter heating bump; peak summer is when instant booking matters most and the callback gap costs the most jobs.
- **Royal-Pawz-style lift math (their dollars):** Assume ~150 booking-intent visitors/month and the callback form converts ~8% = ~12 jobs. True online booking + SMS follow-up at a conservative ~20% = ~30 jobs — **+18 jobs/month**. At a blended ~$450 captured value/job, that's **~$8,000/month / ~$96,000/year** in incremental revenue from conversion alone, before any paid-search scaling. Even a 2x booking-rate lift (8% -> 16%) is ~+12 jobs ≈ +$5,400/month. Layer a managed Google Ads funnel and the upside compounds.

## 8. Pitch Hook & Fit Summary
- **Why them / why now:** A genuinely beloved, Class-A-licensed, 4.8-star/~140-review owner-operator brand throttles its own growth with a "Book Online" button that's really a callback form — and review velocity looks stalled. The reputation (the hard part) is won; the conversion machine is the missing 20%.
- **The lead gap:** No real-time self-scheduling, no SMS follow-up automation, no paid-search engine — every web lead waits on Walter's phone tag while franchise competitors book instantly.
- **Recommended Zapp tier:** **Revenue Diagnostic** first. This is a more established, multi-page, citation-rich operation than the typical "invisible early-stage" lead, so lead with a paid diagnostic that quantifies the booking-form leak and review-velocity decay, then upsell the System Build (real online booking + Twilio SMS + managed Google Ads).
- **Fit score rationale (48/100):** DEMOTED per the established-brand rule — ~140 reviews, 8 years in business, a built-out WordPress SEO site, and active Thumbtack/citation presence make this NOT a thin-infra early-stage prospect. But it's a clean owner-operator with high-ticket economics, an affluent adjacent geo, a reachable decision-maker, and one crisp, fixable conversion leak — a credible if not top-priority fit.

dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & verification

**Legal name:** ALL BRANDS AIR CONDITIONING LLC (VERIFIED — Texas Comptroller).
**Vertical:** Residential HVAC — repair-first AC/heating, installs, ductwork, indoor air quality, mini-splits.
**Service area (VERIFIED from site):** Spring + NW/N Houston corridor — Spring, Tomball, Klein, Cypress, Conroe, The Woodlands, Kingwood, Humble, Atascocita, Shenandoah, plus a long tail east/south (Katy has a dedicated page). Base ZIP **77388** (Spring). This is the **NW Houston/Spring/Klein** market — NOT the affluent Memorial/Energy Corridor/Briar Forest pocket; ROI framing below is adjusted accordingly.
**Texas entity status (VERIFIED — Comptroller, Taxpayer #32100919680):** Right to Transact Business **ACTIVE**; SOS status ACTIVE; **Effective SOS Registration 06/25/2025**; SOS File #0806102670; Registered Agent **Teresa L Cain**; mailing & registered address **3411 La Fleur Ln, Spring, TX 77388** (a residential address — owner-operated from home). PIR "not on file" yet, consistent with a brand-new entity.
**Age reconciliation (diligence flag):** The LLC is **under 1 year old** (06/2025), yet Thumbtack says "3 years in business" and listings cite the owner's "18 years of experience." Most likely: long personal trade experience + ~3 years operating, with the LLC formalized in mid-2025. Treat the **business as genuinely early-stage**.
**License (UNVERIFIED — self-reported):** TDLR **#TACLB147312E**, Class B AC contractor (residential/light-commercial <25 tons), published on the company's About page. Live TDLR status lookup did not complete this session — verify.
**Active confirmation:** Website **live** (VERIFIED). Phone **(832) 663-6160** advertised as primary, "call or text 24/7." Note **phone inconsistency across listings**: Nextdoor page shows (832) 662-3046 and a search snippet surfaced (361) 461-4400 (Corpus Christi area code). Confirm the canonical number live.

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

Strong, consistent 5-star reputation across every platform — but **fragmented across third-party sites, not consolidated on Google or the website.**
- **Thumbtack (UNVERIFIED, single-source):** 4.9★, **72 reviews**, hired 23×, ~1-min response time.
- **Best Pick Reports (UNVERIFIED):** 5.0★, **62 ratings** (61 five-star, 1 four-star), "Best Pick since 2024," tagged family-owned.
- **Angi / HomeAdvisor (UNVERIFIED):** 5.0★ overall (counts not exposed).
- **Nextdoor (UNVERIFIED):** ~5 recommendations; active as recently as Apr 2026 (promo post).
- **Yelp:** listing exists; count not retrievable (403).
- **Google Business Profile:** **review count/rating UNKNOWN — could not confirm this session** (Maps/Yelp blocked). This is the single most important reputation number still open.

**Praise themes (proof the service is worth scaling):** fast response / 24-hr turnaround; honest "repair-first," no upselling/hidden fees; correct diagnosis where competitors failed; punctual, professional, communicative; fair pricing + financing. **Recurring complaints:** none surfaced in available sources (low absolute volume, so absence is weak evidence).

**Demotion check:** 72 Thumbtack + 62 Best Pick reviews is real traction, but it's spread thin across platforms on a <1-yr-old LLC, with an unconfirmed (likely small) Google footprint. This keeps the lead in the **early-stage** band rather than "established incumbent" — no major fit demotion, but the reputation is real enough that the pitch is "convert the demand you already earned," not "build demand from zero."

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

**Website:** allbrandsair.com — built on **Lirp / Duda** templated site builder (VERIFIED via `lirp.cdn-website.com` CDN). Multi-page, mobile-responsive, decent service-page SEO scaffolding, financing CTA.
**The killer defect — fake reviews on the site:** the **/reviews/ page is unedited template placeholder** — 9 identical "**Jane Faber, New York**" / "**Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet…**" 5-star testimonials (VERIFIED via live fetch). The business has 130+ genuine 5-star reviews elsewhere, yet a homeowner who clicks "Reviews" sees obviously fake stock content. This actively **destroys trust at the decision moment.**
**Booking:** **No online booking.** A "Book Online" link exists but routes to the **/contact form** (name/email/phone/address/message). Otherwise phone-only. "Call or text us 24/7" = **manual text-quote handling**, no scheduling app, no calendar.
**Payment:** No online payment; financing is "apply via contact." $69 diagnostic fee (waived w/ repair).
**Automation:** **None.** No SMS/email follow-up, no missed-call text-back, no review-request automation. No Google Analytics or Meta Pixel detected — **zero measurement and zero retargeting infrastructure.**
**Lead-capture quality:** Low. Every lead funnels into a generic contact form or an unmanaged inbox/text thread; no instant confirmation, no nurture, no speed-to-lead system beyond the owner personally answering.

**SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** The path from "interested homeowner" to "booked job" is broken twice — (1) the website's social proof is **fake placeholder text** instead of the real 5-star reviews that would close the sale, and (2) there is **no online booking** to capture intent, only a form/phone that depends on the owner being available. Demand is being generated (great reviews, repeat hires) and then leaking out at the exact conversion step Zapp fixes.

## 4. Acquisition & ad presence

**Google Ads:** No confirmed active campaigns; no pixels on site to support it (UNVERIFIED — confirm with a live SERP check on "ac repair spring tx").
**Meta ads:** **None** — no advertiser match in the Meta Ad Library (VERIFIED; keyword search returns only unrelated national advertisers).
**Pixels:** **No Meta Pixel, no Google Analytics** detected on the live site (VERIFIED) — they cannot currently measure or retarget anything.
**Platform dependency:** Acquisition today is effectively **organic + marketplace** — Thumbtack (paying per lead, 23 hires), Best Pick Reports, Angi/HomeAdvisor, Nextdoor word-of-mouth. They are **renting** leads from aggregators rather than owning a funnel.
**SEO footprint:** Templated service/city pages give modest local coverage, but they are **absent from curated "best HVAC in Spring" lists** (Expertise.com's 7 picks do not include them) and don't rank against the incumbents below. Brand-name searches resolve; competitive head terms do not.

## 5. Owner / decision-maker

**Owner/operator:** **Anthony Cortez** (listed on Thumbtack as **Anthony Earnhart-Cortez**) — UNVERIFIED full name. The brand's entire pitch is owner-direct: "every customer works directly with me, the owner." **Comptroller registered agent is Teresa L Cain** (VERIFIED) — not the owner; likely spouse/family member (consistent with the "family-owned" tag) — confirm the relationship and who actually makes buying decisions.
**Reachability:** Strong. Direct line **(832) 663-6160**, explicitly "call or **text** 24/7," ~1-minute response on Thumbtack, active on Facebook (@AllBrandsAir), Instagram (@allbrandsair), and Nextdoor (posting promos as recently as Apr 2026). This is a **highly reachable, responsive owner-operator** — ideal for Zapp's single-operator engagement model.
**Personality signals:** Values-forward ("honest contractor," repair-first, no upsell), promotion-savvy (financing, seasonal replacement offers), hands-on. Family-owned angle is usable in messaging.

## 6. Competitive landscape

NW Houston HVAC is crowded with review-heavy, ad-savvy incumbents:
- **The Chill Brothers** — ~4.6★ with **~1,183 Google reviews**; heavy paid + SEO; online scheduling. Dominant on volume.
- **Total Comfort A/C Systems (Spring)** — 5.0★ on ~92 Google reviews; strong local SEO.
- **Mr. Chill Heating & Air (Tomball/Klein/Spring)** — established brand, "100+ years experience" positioning, polished site/booking.
- **Hal Watson A/C (60+ yrs)**, **Morrow Mechanical**, **Champion Forest A/C** — entrenched, BBB-accredited, deep review moats.

**What they have that All Brands lacks:** real on-site reviews (not placeholders), online scheduling, tracking/retargeting, active paid search, and 100s–1,000s of Google reviews.
**Where All Brands can realistically win:** the owner-direct, honest-repair-first niche resonates with homeowners burned by big-box upsell shops — and All Brands' actual ratings (4.9–5.0) are **as high or higher** than the incumbents. They don't need more reputation; they need to **surface the reputation they have and capture intent before the homeowner clicks to a competitor.** That's a conversion-infrastructure win, not a brand-building slog.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

**Ticket sizes (Houston HVAC, typical ranges):** diagnostic/repair $150–$600; major repair $800–$2,000; **system replacement $7,000–$14,000+** (the prize — and All Brands pushes financed replacements). Blended revenue per booked job, mixing repairs and a steady share of installs, conservatively **$700–$1,200+**.
**Seasonality:** Sharp Houston summer peak (May–Sept) when AC demand and CAC both spike; secondary winter heating bump. Speed-to-lead and booking friction matter most exactly when demand is highest.
**Paid-search economics (NW Houston HVAC):** clicks on "ac repair near me" run roughly $8–$25; cost-per-lead commonly **$60–$150**; at a healthy booked-lead close rate, blended **CAC ~$150–$400/job** — easily ROAS-positive on a $700–$1,200 job and dramatically so when it converts into a financed replacement.

**Royal-Pawz-style conversion lift (the math, illustrative):** Suppose paid + organic traffic currently drives ~120 funnel visits/month at a ~8% booked rate = ~10 jobs/month. Royal Pawz moved booking conversion from ~7% to ~31%. Even a **conservative doubling to ~16%** at the same traffic = **~19 jobs/month — +9 jobs**. At a blended **$900/job**, that's **~+$8,100/month (~+$97K/yr)** in recovered revenue, before any paid-traffic scale-up — and a single extra **financed replacement** ($7K–$14K) in a month can dwarf that. Fixing the fake-reviews trust gap + adding real online booking is the cheapest lever to pull that lift.

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

**Why them / why now:** A brand-new (06/2025) LLC with a genuinely excellent 4.9–5.0 reputation across Thumbtack/Best Pick/Angi/Nextdoor, a reachable text-back owner — and a website that **shows fake placeholder reviews and has no real online booking.** They've proven the service sells; they're just leaking the demand at the conversion step. It's peak-season Houston (summer), the most expensive time to waste a lead.
**The lead gap:** Real reputation exists but is invisible on their own site; no online booking; no SMS automation; no pixels/measurement; acquisition rented from aggregators.
**Recommended Zapp tier:** **Either.** Lead with a **Revenue Diagnostic** to quantify the booking-rate leak and confirm the live numbers (Google reviews, ad presence), which naturally upsells into a **Revenue System Build** (real-review integration → optimized landing → online booking → Twilio SMS follow-up → pixels). The Build is the obvious end state; the Diagnostic de-risks the entry and gives a concrete ROI number.
**Fit score rationale (74):** Owner-operator ✔, reachable/text-friendly ✔, high-ticket + recurring/financed installs ✔, thin conversion infra ✔ (fake reviews, no booking, no automation, no pixels) — all strong. Held below the 80s by: (a) geo is NW Houston/Spring, not the premium Memorial/Energy-Corridor pocket; (b) reputation already strong enough that the play is conversion, not demand-gen; (c) a very new entity with modest budget signals; (d) unconfirmed Google footprint.
**Diligence flags:** Verify TDLR license status live; confirm owner identity (Cortez vs. registered agent Teresa Cain); reconcile the three phone numbers; confirm Google review count/rating; confirm whether they're actively buying Google Ads.

dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & verification

- **Legal name:** Mama Bear Pest & Outdoor LLC (VERIFIED; appears as "MAMA BEAR PEST AND OUTDOOR LLC" in the TDA structural-pest license directory and "Mama Bear Pest & Outdoor" on the brand site).
- **Vertical:** Residential pest control + wildlife removal/exclusion + attic insulation. Service lines: general pest, green/organic pest, mosquito, termite, rodent (rats/mice), wildlife trapping/exclusion, attic. (VERIFIED via site nav.)
- **Service area:** Katy and surrounding West Houston (VERIFIED). Listed business address 22627 Leedstown Ln, Katy, TX 77449 (VERIFIED across site, Yelp, license) -- this is a residential address in the 77449 ZIP (NW Katy), consistent with a home-run operator. Note: 77449 itself is a more mid-market Katy ZIP; their *marketing* reach into affluent West Houston (Energy Corridor/Memorial/Briar Forest) is the upside, not where they're headquartered.
- **Texas entity status / formation date:** NOT confirmed this session. The Comptroller franchise account-status search is JavaScript-gated and could not be submitted via fetch; no Secretary of State record was retrieved. **Proxy evidence**: TDA license issued 2024-02-28; press release dated Sept 12, 2024; About page says the venture "began three years ago." Best estimate: LLC formed ~2023-2024, operating roughly 2-3 years. (UNVERIFIED exact date -- flagged for live check.)
- **License + status:** TDA Structural Pest Control License (TPCL) **#0928581** (directory shows "928581") -- **ACTIVE**, categories **General Pest Control + Termite Control**, responsible certified applicator **JOHN M HARRINGTON**, issued **2024-02-28**, expires **2027-02-28**, Harris County. (VERIFIED via verifiedpestcontrol.com directory pulling TDA data.) No disciplinary flags found.
- **Active confirmation:** Site is **live** (mamabearpest.com, HTTPS). Phone **(281) 656-1525** is consistently published across site, Yelp, Nextdoor, license record (VERIFIED valid as the canonical business line; not test-dialed). Email **office@mamabearpest.com** (VERIFIED). Hours Mon-Fri 8a-6p.

The seed claims check out with one important correction: **the seed's "owner John" is only half right.** John M Harrington is the licensed operator/lead inspector; **Leda Harrington is the owner/CEO**, which is what makes the "woman-owned" brand claim legitimate.

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

- **Google:** Rating **5.0 stars** (VERIFIED via the Google Maps listing). The **exact review count could not be confirmed** -- Google Maps blocks automated reads, and the site only advertises "100% 5-Star Google Rating" with no number. This is the single most important number to confirm live. Given business age, the count is almost certainly low (single digits to low double digits), which is *consistent with early stage* -- no demotion warranted. (Count UNVERIFIED.)
- **Yelp:** Listing exists ("Mama Bear Pest & Outdoor," 22627 Leedstown Ln). A search summary returned "5.0 with ~1 review." (UNVERIFIED -- single source; Yelp 403'd automated fetch.) Effectively no Yelp traction.
- **HomeAdvisor / Angi:** ~**8 reviews at 4.4** on the main HomeAdvisor/Angi listing; a second Angi listing shows 5.0. The 4.4 (vs 5.0 on Google) is the only sub-perfect signal -- worth reading those 1-2 lower reviews live. (UNVERIFIED counts.)
- **Nextdoor:** Page exists; genuine neighbor recommendations ("wonderful for an exterminator," "Family run business and they use clean products," Oct 2024) but no numeric recommendation count surfaced. Nextdoor is a real, warm channel for this brand.
- **Facebook:** Active page (facebook.com/mamabearpestcontrol). Seed note says ~169 likes (UNVERIFIED). Has at least one "big news" announcement post (content blocked to fetch -- likely the GorillaDesk/online-scheduling or financing launch).
- **Instagram @mamabearpest:** 72 followers / 127 posts (UNVERIFIED, single source) -- high post count vs tiny following = they're *producing content but it isn't converting to reach*.

**Review THEMES (proof the service is worth scaling):** recurring praise for honesty / no high-pressure upsell, eco-friendly/family-safe (plant-based) products, communication, thoroughness (checking entry points), and a 1-year warranty -- explicitly contrasted by reviewers against "year-long-contract" national chains. **Recurring complaint themes:** none material surfaced; the only soft signal is the 4.4 (vs 5.0) on HomeAdvisor/Angi, unread. The differentiation story (honest, eco, family/woman-owned, no lock-in contracts) is a *strong, scalable* positioning -- the problem is volume of people seeing it, not the quality.

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

- **Website / platform:** WordPress + **Elementor** page builder, built/maintained by agency **NertzDigital** (VERIFIED via /wp-content/, elementor/thumbs artifacts and footer credit). Reasonably full site (service silo pages for every pest, service-area pages, About, Financing) -- better than a typical UENI/templated one-pager. Mobile experience presumed responsive (Elementor default) -- confirm live.
- **Tracking:** Google Analytics / gtag present. **No Meta Pixel detected** (so no Facebook retargeting in place). **No Twilio / chat widget / SMS-automation script detected.** (VERIFIED-from-source.)
- **Booking -- the crux:** Split path. **NEW customers -> /book-now is a plain contact form** (fields: name, phone, email+confirm, street/city/ZIP, "how did you hear," service description). **No date/time picker, no calendar, no instant confirmation, no online payment.** **RETURNING customers -> /schedule links out to a GorillaDesk customer portal** (login required) where existing clients can view/pay invoices and request a follow-up. So the business *owns real field-service software (GorillaDesk) but only exposes it to people who are already customers.* New, cold, ad-driven traffic cannot self-book a time -- they submit a form and wait for a manual callback.
- **Payment:** GorillaDesk handles invoicing/online payment for existing customers; financing is offered via a third party (**Integrity Payments**, "apply in 4 easy steps"). New customers see no upfront online payment/deposit. (VERIFIED.)
- **Follow-up automation:** No SMS/email nurture detected on-site. GorillaDesk can send appointment/text reminders, but there's no evidence of a speed-to-lead auto-text on the new-lead form. (UNVERIFIED-negative -- confirm.)
- **Lead-capture quality:** The form captures the right fields (address + problem), which is good. But it's a *batch-and-callback* model, not a *book-the-job-now* model.

**SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** The new-customer front door is a no-calendar contact form. Every cold visitor and every future ad click lands on "fill out a form and we'll get back to you," with no time-slot selection, no instant confirmation, and no automated speed-to-lead text. This is exactly the Royal Pawz 7%-conversion starting condition -- the demand is real, but the path from interested-visitor to booked-appointment leaks.

## 4. Acquisition & ad presence

- **Google Ads:** None detected (no sponsored result observed for their terms; no gads/conversion tag confirmed). (UNVERIFIED-negative -- confirm in Ads Transparency Center.)
- **Meta ads:** No Meta Pixel on site, which strongly implies no active Meta advertising/retargeting. Meta Ad Library check was inconclusive (fetch failed) -- confirm live. (Largely UNVERIFIED-negative.)
- **Platform dependency:** Present on HomeAdvisor/Angi (the ~8-review/4.4 listing suggests they've taken Angi/HA leads) and Nextdoor (organic word-of-mouth). Light Yelp footprint, no Yelp Ads evident. They are **not** heavily locked into any single paid platform -- meaning a Zapp-owned acquisition channel is greenfield, not a rescue.
- **SEO footprint:** They have a proper service-silo WordPress site (good on-page structure) but in a brutally competitive Katy pest market dominated by 70-year, 1,000+-review incumbents, they almost certainly do NOT rank on page one for head terms like "pest control Katy." Organic discovery is minimal; growth today is word-of-mouth + Nextdoor + directory leads.

**Net:** essentially **zero owned paid-acquisition engine.** Demand is currently inbound/referral. That's the gap Zapp fills.

## 5. Owner / decision-maker

- **Leda Harrington -- Owner / CEO** (VERIFIED via press release + About page). The "mom/Mama Bear" founder; she is the woman-owned, family-health, eco-conscious brand voice. She is the economic decision-maker and the emotional buyer for a "help me grow my family business" pitch.
- **John M Harrington -- Lead Inspector + TDA responsible certified applicator** (VERIFIED, TPCL #0928581). He's the technical/operations half and the licensed name on the business.
- **Contact:** office@mamabearpest.com; (281) 656-1525. LinkedIn company page exists; no strong personal LinkedIn surfaced. They publish on Instagram/Facebook/YouTube (content-willing but low reach).
- **Personality signals:** Brand voice is warm, anti-upsell, family-and-pet-safety forward, explicitly positions *against* big-chain annual contracts. Construction background ("stop pests at the source"). They reply to / cultivate Nextdoor word-of-mouth. **Angle: a woman-owned, family-operated, eco-positioned local underdog that already has a differentiated story and happy customers -- ideal "you have the product, you're missing the machine" pitch.**

## 6. Competitive landscape

West Houston / Katy pest is dominated by SEO-and-ad-heavy incumbents:
- **Modern Pest Control (getmypests.com)** -- 70+ years, family-owned, **1,000+ five-star reviews**, full online presence. Owns the head SEO terms and brand trust.
- **EnviroCon Pest Control (Katy)** -- established, strong multi-year retention reviews, professional web funnel.
- **ABC Home & Commercial Services** -- large regional player with easy online scheduling, fleets, and broad ad spend.
- (Also Bugs Incorporated -- multiple "Best of Katy" wins; Barefoot Mosquito & Pest.)

**What they have that Mama Bear lacks:** hundreds-to-thousands of reviews, page-one rankings, real online scheduling on the *new-customer* path, and active paid acquisition.
**Where Mama Bear can realistically win:** the *underdog/eco/woman-owned/no-contract* niche; hyper-local Katy/West Houston targeting the affluent residential pockets (Energy Corridor, Briar Forest, Memorial) where homeowners pay for family-safe, honest, responsive service and dislike big-chain lock-in. The incumbents win on scale; Mama Bear can win on trust + speed if -- and only if -- the booking path stops leaking and a paid channel feeds it.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

- **Ticket sizes (West Houston pest, typical ranges):** one-time general pest service ~$125-$250; **recurring quarterly plans ~$120-$175 per visit (~$400-$700/yr LTV+)**; rodent/exclusion jobs ~$300-$1,500; termite treatments ~$800-$2,500; mosquito seasonal packages ~$400-$700. Recurring plans are the prize -- they convert a one-off into annual recurring revenue.
- **Paid-search economics (this vertical, West Houston):** pest-control Google Ads CPCs run high (~$8-$25+/click on commercial-intent terms); blended cost-per-lead commonly ~$40-$120, and **cost-per-booked-job is dominated by the booking conversion rate** -- which is exactly the lever Zapp pulls. At a 7%-style form conversion, paid traffic is unaffordable; at a 25-30% booking rate, the same spend becomes profitable.
- **Seasonality:** strong spring-summer surge (mosquitoes, ants, wasps Apr-Sep) and a rodent push in fall/winter -- so a paid engine launched now (June) hits peak demand.
- **Royal-Pawz-style lift math (illustrative, their dollars):** Say a modest paid + GBP program drives **100 new-customer site visits/month**. At today's form-and-callback ~7-10% booking rate that's ~7-10 jobs. Lifting to a Royal-Pawz-style ~25-30% booking rate => **~25-30 jobs/month** from the same traffic. At a conservative **$200 blended first-job value**, that's a swing from ~$1,600 to ~$5,500/month -- **~$3,500-$4,000+/month in incremental revenue**, before counting recurring-plan LTV (each converted recurring customer is worth $400-$700+/yr). Even half that lift pays for the engine many times over. The point of leverage isn't more traffic first -- it's that *they're already throwing away most of the interested visitors they get.*

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

- **Why them / why now (one line):** A woman-owned, eco-positioned Katy pest startup with genuinely happy customers and a 5.0 Google rating, heading into peak pest season with NO paid-acquisition engine and a front door (new-customer contact form) that leaks every cold lead -- they already bought field-service software (GorillaDesk) but only point it at existing clients.
- **The lead gap:** New leads can't self-book a time; there's no instant confirmation and no speed-to-lead automation; no Google/Meta engine feeds the pipe; reviews are thin so organic discovery is near-zero.
- **Recommended Zapp tier:** **Revenue System Build.** The diagnosis is already clear (no front-door booking, no paid channel, thin reviews) -- they don't need a paid audit to find the problem, they need the machine built: Google Ads -> a dedicated high-intent landing page -> *real* new-customer online booking (extend the GorillaDesk they already own to the front door) -> Twilio SMS speed-to-lead + reminders + review-request loop (which also fixes the thin-review problem). This is almost a carbon copy of the Royal Pawz playbook.
- **Fit score rationale (82/100):** Owner-operator with clear decision-maker (Leda) = high. Thin conversion infra at the front door with software already in-house to extend = very high (easy, fast win). High-ticket + recurring-revenue vertical = high. Reachable (phone/email/social, content-willing) = high. Affluent-geo upside (West Houston/Energy Corridor/Memorial) = strong. Minor drags: HQ ZIP 77449 is mid-market (their *target* geo, not location, is the affluent play); tiny ad/social reach today means more build-from-zero than rescue-existing-spend; and the unconfirmed exact Google review count slightly limits certainty (though early-stage thin reviews actually *raise* fit). No demotion triggered -- this is genuinely an early-stage, established-product, under-marketed operator, which is the bullseye profile.
- **Diligence flags:** (1) Confirm exact Google review count + oldest-review date live (drives both the pitch and the fit certainty). (2) Read the HomeAdvisor/Angi 4.4 reviews for any service complaint before pitching "scale it." (3) Confirm LLC franchise status is active (Comptroller). (4) Confirm no paid ads are already running (Ads Transparency / Meta Ad Library) so the "you have no engine" hook is accurate.

## 1. Identity & verification

- **Service area:** Site lists Houston plus Spring, Sugar Land, Bellaire, Deer Park, La Porte, Pasadena, Webster, Pearland, Baytown, Katy, Friendswood, League City, Kingwood, Clear Lake. Site states they "operate from the South Houston area," and TikTok content tags #cypresstx. (VERIFIED — contact/full-detail pages.) **CORRECTION to seed:** the seed said "serves west side"; the published footprint is metro-wide and explicitly anchored to **South Houston**, not the west side. Formation date: **unknown** (no entity record; brand appears to be recently established based on thin review/social footprint).
- **License + status:** Mobile auto detailing requires **no Texas state occupational license** (no TDLR/TDA/TCEQ license applies to basic detailing). n/a. No license flags. (VERIFIED — vertical is unlicensed in TX.)
- **Active confirmation:** Site is live (Squarespace). Phone/text (346) 704-8983 published on every page; email hienergydetailing@gmail.com active. GBP live ("Closed · Opens 9 AM Mon"). TikTok posting within ~3 weeks of research date. Business is **active and currently operating.** (VERIFIED.)

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

- **Google Business Profile:** **5.0 stars, 16 reviews** — "Car detailing service," phone (346) 704-8983, hours shown. GBP exists and is **claimed/active** (it carries hours, category, website link). (VERIFIED — Google local pack, live this session.) Their own homepage points customers to "our Google profile (High Energy Detailing)" for 5-star reviews, confirming the GBP is theirs.

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

- **Website:** highenergydetailing.com, built on **Squarespace** (templated). Clean, mobile-responsive, multi-page (home, full detail, interior tiers, clay/seal, ceramic coating, headlight restoration, contact). Better-looking than a typical UENI/GoDaddy one-pager, but it is a **brochure, not a conversion machine.** (VERIFIED platform = Squarespace.)
- **Pricing transparency:** Partial — "Full Detail starting at $197," "Ceramic Coating starting at $399." Helpful, but no per-vehicle-size grid and no firm quote without contact. (VERIFIED.)
- **Booking flow:** **Quote/contact form ONLY.** Every CTA is "Get Free Quote" → /contact ("Fill out some info and we'll be in touch shortly"). There is **no online scheduling, no calendar, no real-time availability, no instant price-and-book.** Customer must submit a form and **wait for a manual reply**, or call/text. (VERIFIED across home, full-detail, ceramic, contact pages.)
- **Payment:** **Cash, Zelle, Cash App only** (per search-surfaced site copy). **No online/card payment, no deposit capture.** (VERIFIED via search extract; site itself does not always display this — confirm live.) This blocks card-preferring affluent customers and any pay-to-hold-the-slot mechanic.
- **Follow-up automation:** **None detected.** No evidence of SMS or email automation, no booking-confirmation system, no review-request automation — follow-up is whatever the owner manually texts. (UNVERIFIED — inferred from form-only stack; no automation tooling visible.)
- **Lead-capture quality:** Low-to-medium. A form that promises "we'll be in touch shortly" leaks every lead that wants to book *now*, every after-hours lead (hours Mon–Sat 9–5, closed Sunday — and Sunday is prime car-care time), and every price-shopper comparing against competitors who let them book in two clicks.

In a market where the top West Houston competitors offer click-to-schedule with real-time availability, High Energy's "fill out a form and wait" flow is the exact 7%→30.8% gap Zapp closed for Royal Pawz. ## 5. Owner / decision-maker

- **Name:** **Unknown / not publicly disclosed.** No owner or founder name appears on the website, GBP, Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, or any directory, and no TX entity registration exists to reveal a registered agent/officer. I will not guess. (Searched by brand, phone, and "owner/founder" — no name surfaced.)

Reads as a young, energetic, marketing-aware solo operator who is comfortable on camera — exactly the profile that says yes to a done-for-you growth system because they already believe in marketing but lack the conversion/ads infrastructure. (UNVERIFIED inference from public content.)
- **Family-/woman-owned/bilingual angle:** None identified. (Unknown.)

## 6. Competitive landscape

West/SW Houston mobile detailing is crowded and several incumbents are SEO- and booking-mature:

1. **Houston Auto Details** (houstonautodetails.com) — explicitly markets Memorial/Energy Corridor, runs **online booking via Goldie (book.heygoldie.com)** with visible availability, strong SEO, ceramic-coating positioning. Has what High Energy lacks: real-time online scheduling + page-one SEO. (VERIFIED booking platform.)
2. **Azul Auto Detailing** (azulautodetailing.com) — "easy online booking system," concierge framing, serves River Oaks/Memorial/Katy/Sugar Land — directly overlapping affluent zones. (VERIFIED online booking claim.)
3. **Car Detailing HTX** (cardetailinghtx.com) — "simply book online," published pricing pages, eco/waterless angle, strong SEO. (VERIFIED online booking.)
4. Supporting pack: 4K Mobile Auto Detailing, R3 Detailing ("200 5-star reviews"), Rhythm, Hypergloss — all with deeper review counts and/or booking.

**What incumbents have that High Energy lacks:** real-time online booking, card payment, page-one SEO, and (for R3) 100–200+ reviews. **Where High Energy can realistically win:** it already has the hardest-to-fake asset — **5.0/5 work quality and engaging content** — but is throttled by a quote-form funnel and zero paid demand-capture. Bolt on Google Ads → a fast landing page → online booking + deposit, and it converts the demand it's already generating instead of leaking it to competitors who simply make it easier to book. The competitive gap is **infrastructure, not craftsmanship** — the ideal Zapp setup.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

- **Ticket size (Houston, 2026):** Interior detail ~$130–300; full detail ~$179–550 (their own Full Detail starts at $197); ceramic coating $399 (theirs) up to $500–2,000+ for multi-year/multi-layer; SUVs/trucks +$50–100. Blended realistic **average ticket ≈ $225–300**, with ceramic/correction jobs running $400–1,000+. (VERIFIED market ranges; their starting prices VERIFIED.)
- **Paid-search economics (this vertical, West Houston):** Auto-detailing search CPCs typically run ~$3–8; with a tuned landing page + booking, a well-run campaign commonly lands **CAC in the ~$40–90 range** per booked job. At a $225–300 ticket (and high-margin ceramic upsells), that supports a comfortable **3–5x ROAS** once the funnel converts — the same envelope Zapp hit with Royal Pawz. (UNVERIFIED — industry estimate, not a measured figure for this business.)
- **Seasonality:** Spring/summer peak (pollen, love-bug, road-trip prep) in Houston; demand softens in deep winter but the Gulf climate keeps detailing fairly year-round. Plan ad spend to lean into Mar–Sep. (UNVERIFIED general pattern.)
- **Royal-Pawz-style ROI math (simple, illustrative — NOT a promise):**
  - Assume the form-only funnel converts site/ad visitors at ~7% (Royal Pawz's starting point) and the same traffic at ~25–30% once it's "click-to-book + deposit + SMS reminder."
  - Say paid + organic drives **120 qualified clicks/month** to the page. At 7% → ~8 booked jobs. At 28% → ~34 booked jobs. That's **+26 jobs/month**.
  - At a conservative **$250 average ticket**, +26 jobs ≈ **+$6,500/month ≈ +$78k/year** — before counting ceramic-coating upsells (each $399–1,000+) that the current "wait for a text" flow routinely loses to faster competitors.
  - Even a far more modest doubling (7%→15%) on the same traffic is roughly **+$2,000–2,500/month**. The point: the work quality already converts at 5.0 stars; the bottleneck is purely letting people **book and pay instantly.**

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

- **Why them / why now (one line):** A 5.0-star, content-savvy solo detailer who is *already creating demand* on TikTok/Instagram but pours every lead into a "fill out a form and wait for a text" funnel with no online booking, no card payment, and no paid demand-capture — the exact conversion + acquisition gap Zapp closed for Royal Pawz.

dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & verification

**Legal name:** I & J Pressure Washing LLC (VERIFIED — consistent across own website, Yelp, Nextdoor, HomeAdvisor, Central Index). The "I & J" almost certainly references two principals' first initials (owner-operator pair, likely family/spouse).

**Vertical:** Residential + light-commercial pressure washing / exterior cleaning. Service menu (VERIFIED from site): soft washing, house washing, roof washing, driveways/flatwork, fence cleaning, gutter cleaning & brightening, window cleaning, pool/patio, sealing, playgrounds, trash pads & parking lots, farm equipment. Tagline: "No job is too small or too big" / "Family-owned."

**Service area:** Base address 1906 Kingston Meadow Lane, Katy, TX 77494 (VERIFIED — Yelp + Central Index; this is a residential address = home-based operator). Site lists "Greater Houston, Katy, Brookshire, Cinco Ranch, Fleshier [Fulshear], Richmond, Sealy" (VERIFIED on site). 77494 is far-west Katy/Cinco Ranch — solidly affluent suburban.

**Texas entity status + formation date:** Entity is real and operating as an LLC, but exact TX SOS file number and formation date are **UNKNOWN** — the Texas Comptroller franchise "Account Status" search and Bizapedia are JS/bot-walled and returned no record via available tools. All review activity dates to mid-2024, so formation is **inferred ~2023–2024 (UNVERIFIED)**. *Live check required to confirm ACTIVE good-standing.*

**License + status:** n/a — Texas does NOT license residential pressure/soft washing (no TDLR, TCEQ, or pest-TPCL trade license applies). This is a structural fact, **not** a gap or red flag (VERIFIED).

**Active confirmation:** Site is LIVE (fetched this session). Phone (214) 566-7183 present everywhere (VERIFIED listed). Note: **214 is a Dallas area code**, not Houston/Katy 281/832/713 — owner likely relocated from DFW; a Katy prospect using a Dallas cell is a mild trust/SEO-locality friction point worth confirming.

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

**The numbers (handle with care):** The website and Yelp both display **5.0 stars / "24 reviews"** — but this "24" is the **UENI review-widget count**, not a confirmed Google Business Profile count. On the actual /reviews page only ~6 reviews render individually, and **five of them are dated the exact same day — Jul 22, 2024** (jesus leon, Adrienne Black, Carlos, Maurissa Rosario, Dominey Osborne), with one more on Aug 30, 2024 (Troy DZ). A same-day cluster of five 5-star reviews is the classic signature of a **seeded / friends-and-family launch batch**, not 24 distinct organic reviews. So: real social proof exists, but the "24" should be treated as **UNVERIFIED and likely inflated by the widget**. This directly confirms the draft note "reviews only from 2024."

**Other platforms:** Nextdoor page shows **"8 recommendations"** (UNVERIFIED, single-source). HomeAdvisor profile exists (free estimates, credit cards accepted) but was 403-blocked so its review count is unconfirmed. Facebook page (@IJpressurewashingllc) exists but is login-walled — follower/recommendation counts not retrievable via tools. No confirmed independent **Google** review count was surfaced; **GBP claim status UNKNOWN** (top live-check priority).

**Review THEMES (proof the service is worth scaling):** Uniformly positive — "great job cleaning the exterior… very professional, prompt, and easy to work with," "excellent service and attention to detail," "made my fence look brand new," "professional… quality work." A Facebook post from Two Threads Embroidery calls I & J "one of our favorite clients!" (UNVERIFIED context — suggests they invested in branded apparel/uniforms, a small professionalism signal). **No recurring complaints found.** Takeaway: the work quality and customer experience are clearly good; the constraint is purely volume/reach/conversion infrastructure, not service quality — the ideal Zapp profile.

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

**Website / platform:** ijpressurewashingkaty.com is built on **UENI** — VERIFIED beyond doubt: the contact form reads "I agree with the Terms & Conditions and the Privacy & Cookies Policy of **UENI**…" and the site carries the generic UENI "powered by" chrome, auto-generated "We Offer" service tiles, and a boilerplate "Recommended Local Businesses" cross-link page. This is the cheapest, most templated DIY small-business builder there is — minimal SEO control, generic structure, shared template artifacts (note even copy errors: "East Texas"/"Fleshier" typos, mismatched regional references).

**Booking:** **Form-only / manual.** The single conversion mechanism is a "Get In Touch" form that literally states **"An email will be sent to the owner."** Site also says **"By appointment only."** There is **NO online scheduler, NO instant/online quote tool, NO calendar**. A prospect must fill a form (or call the 214 number) and then wait for the owner to manually reply and arrange a quote. (VERIFIED)

**Payment:** "Debit & Credit cards accepted" (VERIFIED on site) — but this is in-person/manual; there is **no online checkout or deposit capture**.

**SMS/email follow-up automation:** **None detected.** No evidence of any Twilio-style SMS, no email nurture, no missed-call text-back. Lead capture is a raw email-to-owner with no CRM, no auto-response, no speed-to-lead system. (UNVERIFIED-negative, but nothing on the UENI template supports it.)

**Lead-capture quality:** Low. Form fields are basic (name/email/phone/message); no qualification, no instant confirmation, no booking commitment. Mobile experience is the generic UENI responsive template — functional but undifferentiated, with the CTA buried below service tiles.

**SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** **There is no frictionless path from "interested" to "booked."** A motivated Katy homeowner lands, sees 5 stars, wants a quote tonight — and the only option is "email the owner and wait." Every lead that doesn't get a fast manual callback leaks to a competitor with a "Book Now" button (Rolling Suds) or an instant quote. This is the textbook Royal-Pawz problem: great service, 7%-style conversion infrastructure.

## 4. Acquisition & ad presence

**Google Ads:** **None detected** (UNVERIFIED-negative — confirm in live SERP). No paid markers found in any search.

**Meta Ad Library:** No active ads surfaced for the Facebook Page (login-wall blocked direct Ad Library confirmation — flag for live check).

**Platform dependency:** Present-but-passive on HomeAdvisor, Yelp, Nextdoor — but no evidence of **paid** Yelp Ads / Angi leads / Thumbtack / HomeAdvisor pay-per-lead. Appears to run on organic word-of-mouth + the seeded review base.

**Pixels:** The UENI template uses reCAPTCHA/Google Privacy ToS; no evidence of a Meta Pixel or Google Ads conversion tag wired for remarketing (UNVERIFIED — verify in live page source).

**SEO footprint:** Weak. UENI sites rarely rank for competitive "pressure washing Katy" head terms, and I & J did **not** appear among the organic SEO leaders for the core term — that space is owned by Rolling Suds, Pressure Washing Katy, Pressure King Softwash, KTX. I & J is essentially **invisible in organic search** for its money keywords; it surfaces only on brand-name and directory searches. They are demand-takers, not demand-generators.

## 5. Owner / decision-maker

**Name(s):** Primary contact strongly indicated as **Abel Bonilla** (UNVERIFIED-but-strong — HomeAdvisor profile URL is rated.**AbelBonilla**.113837182, and review references mention "Abel coming out"). The "I & J" branding implies a **second principal** (the I- or J-named partner — likely spouse/family given "Family-owned"); that second name is **NOT confirmed**. Hispanic/family-owned, **likely bilingual (English/Spanish)** angle — a genuine differentiator for marketing to Katy's large Spanish-speaking homeowner base (UNVERIFIED, infer from name).

**Direct contact:** (214) 566-7183; UENI-masked email on site. No LinkedIn or personal professional presence surfaced.

**Personality signals:** Customers describe the owner as professional, prompt, responsive in person. Engaged enough to seed reviews, set up listings on 5+ platforms, and buy branded embroidery — signs of an owner who **takes the business seriously but is doing all the marketing himself, manually**. This is the ideal owner-operator psychographic for a done-for-you offer: cares about the brand, but has no time/skill for the conversion stack.

## 6. Competitive landscape

**Rolling Suds of Katy–Cypress** — national franchise, professionally built site with **per-zip SEO landing pages** (77494/77449/77447/Magnolia, etc.), truck-mounted hot-water systems, "Book Now!" online scheduling, 100% satisfaction guarantee, active Facebook. Has everything I & J lacks: online booking, deep local SEO, ad budget. The dominant threat.

**Pressure Washing Katy (pressurewashingkaty.com)** and **Pressure King Softwash** — SEO-optimized independents ranking on the head term, with proper service-area content and stronger sites.

**KTX Window Cleaning** — "Voted Best of Katy" multi-year, strong brand/review moat in the overlapping window/exterior niche.

**What they have that I & J lacks:** online booking/instant quote, real organic SEO rankings, paid-ad presence, and (for the franchises) review volume in the hundreds. **Where I & J can realistically win:** it already has the hardest thing to fake — a clean 5.0 reputation and genuinely happy customers — plus a family-owned/bilingual, "no job too small" local angle. Bolt a Royal-Pawz-style acquisition system (ads → fast landing page → online booking → SMS follow-up) onto that authentic service quality and it can capture the high-intent 77494 searches that currently flow straight to Rolling Suds.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

**Ticket size (West Houston pressure washing, typical ranges):** house/soft wash $250–$600; driveway/flatwork $100–$300; full-exterior packages (house + driveway + gutters) $500–$900+; roof soft-wash $400–$900. A blended residential job ≈ **$350–$450**.

**Paid-search economics:** Pressure-washing CPCs in West Houston run roughly **$5–$12/click**; at a realistic 8–12% landing-page booking rate, CAC lands around **$80–$160 per booked job** — comfortably profitable on a $350–$450 ticket, and strongly profitable once repeat/seasonal customers and add-on services stack. Well-run campaigns in this vertical clear **4–6x ROAS**, in line with the Royal Pawz 5x proof point.

**Seasonality:** Spring/early-summer (Mar–Jun) is peak demand in Houston (pollen, mildew, pre-summer curb-appeal); a second bump pre-holidays (Oct–Nov). Right now (June) is **peak season** — timely to launch.

**Royal-Pawz-style lift, in THEIR dollars:** Say I & J currently nets ~15 jobs/month from word-of-mouth at a ~$400 avg = **~$6,000/mo**. The leak isn't traffic quality, it's conversion. Royal Pawz roughly **4x'd** booking conversion (7% → 30.8%). Even a conservative **2x** on captured leads — via ads driving traffic to a fast landing page with real online booking + SMS follow-up — plausibly takes them from 15 to **30+ booked jobs/month = ~$12,000/mo**, a **~$6,000/mo (~$72k/yr) incremental** swing, before counting higher-ticket bundles. The targeting sweet spot is affluent **Cinco Ranch / Cross Creek Ranch / west-Memorial-adjacent 77494** homeowners who pay full price and re-book seasonally — not price-shoppers. The math easily covers a Zapp build + retainer.

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

**Why them / why now:** A genuinely 5-star, family-owned operator sitting on a DIY UENI template with **no online booking, no instant quote, no follow-up automation, and no ad presence** — heading into peak pressure-washing season while a national franchise (Rolling Suds) eats the "Book Now" searches in their exact zip. The service is already good; only the acquisition-and-conversion machine is missing. That is precisely the Zapp wheelhouse and the Royal Pawz pattern.

**The lead gap:** "Email the owner and wait" + "by appointment only" is the entire conversion path. No speed-to-lead, no booking, no nurture.

**Recommended Zapp tier:** **Revenue System Build.** The diagnosis is already obvious; the value is in shipping the full stack — Google Ads → optimized 77494 landing page → online booking → Twilio SMS follow-up — on top of an owner who clearly cares but is doing everything by hand.

**Fit score rationale (82 / STRONG):** Owner-operator ✓; thin conversion infra (UENI form-only, no booking/automation) ✓; high-ticket + repeatable/seasonal recurring revenue ✓; reachable (phone, email, FB, Nextdoor) ✓; affluent geo (77494 Cinco Ranch) ✓; not yet running ads (greenfield) ✓. Held just under mid-80s by two diligence items: the "24 reviews / 5.0" is a UENI widget figure with a same-day seeded cluster (so reputation is real but thinner and less Google-anchored than it looks — this actually *raises* the need for Zapp, not lowers it), and the entity/GBP/owner specifics need live confirmation.

**Diligence flags:** (1) Confirm a claimed Google Business Profile and the TRUE Google review count — the headline "24" is widget-sourced and partly seeded. (2) Confirm TX entity is ACTIVE + get formation date. (3) 214 Dallas area code on a Katy business — confirm owner is local and committed. (4) Nail down both principals' names + a direct mobile.
