dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & Verification

**Legal/brand name.** Operates as "Emerald Lawn Care, LLC" on its Facebook page (facebook.com/EmeraldLawnCare.TX) (VERIFIED brand). The "LLC" suffix and any actual Texas registration could NOT be confirmed against a primary source this session — OpenCorporates was captcha-gated and the Texas Comptroller franchise-tax account-status search requires an interactive form submission (deferred to live pass). Treat the legal entity as **(UNVERIFIED)**.

**Vertical.** Residential lawn care: mowing/edging, mulch installation, flower-bed prep and maintenance, weed removal, light landscaping (VERIFIED from Nextdoor + Yelp service listings).

**Service area — IMPORTANT CORRECTION to the seed.** The seed listed "Houston." Multiple directory listings (Nextdoor, Yelp, eLocal, YellowPages) place the business in **Hockley, TX 77447**, with a directory street address of **17334 Hartford Field Ln, Hockley** and a stated service area of **Hockley, Cypress, and surrounding NW Houston** (VERIFIED service area = Hockley/Cypress; the street address is single-source directory data, **UNVERIFIED**). This is far-NW exurban Harris/Waller County — NOT the affluent Memorial / Briar Forest / Energy Corridor corridor. The "Houston TX" tag on Facebook is a metro label, not a city-proper location. This materially changes the ROI geography (see Section 7).

**Phone / email — active confirmation.** Phone **(713) 904-0575** and email **EmeraldLawnCare.TX@gmail.com** appear consistently across Facebook, Nextdoor, Yelp, and eLocal (VERIFIED as the business's published contact across 4+ sources). A Gmail address (not a domain email) reinforces the "no real marketing site" thesis. Phone was not test-dialed this session.

**Texas entity status + formation date.** Not found from a primary source. **(UNVERIFIED / unknown.)**

**License + status.** Lawn mowing / mulch / bed work in Texas generally requires **no state occupational license** (TDLR does not license basic lawn maintenance). A pesticide/herbicide **TPCL (Texas Dept. of Agriculture)** license would be required only if they apply restricted/commercial weed-and-feed chemicals for hire. Nextdoor/Yelp copy mentions "weed removal" and one AI-aggregated snippet mentioned "fertilizing / weed and lawn pest control," but that snippet appears contaminated by an out-of-state namesake; no TDA TPCL number was found. License status **(UNVERIFIED / likely n/a for basic mowing)**.

**Conflicting tenure claims.** Search snippets variously claimed "family-owned over 10 years" and "40+ years of experience." The "40+ years" line traces to an unrelated **Western New York** "Emerald Lawn Care" (emeraldlawncare.net, 716 area code) and a **Clay, NY** namesake (BizRatings, owner Paul Granato) — NOT this business. Tenure is therefore **(UNVERIFIED)**.

## 2. Reputation & Demand Proof

**Google Business Profile / Google reviews.** No Google review count or rating could be confirmed this session, and no clearly-claimed GBP was surfaced in search. **(UNVERIFIED / unknown — confirm live.)**

**Yelp.** A Yelp listing exists: "Emerald Lawn Care — Hockley, TX, Lawn Services, **125 Photos**, 'Request a Quote'" (VERIFIED listing exists; 125 photos VERIFIED from the result title). The **star rating and review count were NOT extractable** (Yelp returned 403 to all automated fetches). The presence of 125 photos but a "Request a Quote" CTA (rather than a Yelp rating badge in the snippet) suggests a **photo-heavy but thin-review** profile — consistent with an owner-operator who documents jobs but has not harvested reviews. **(Review count UNVERIFIED — confirm live.)**

**Facebook.** Page is the primary presence (facebook.com/EmeraldLawnCare.TX). One search summary cited "189 likes" (single-source, **UNVERIFIED**). Page content is described as job-photo posts and service promos (e.g., mulch installation with "full bed cleanup and HOA compliance support") — classic owner-operator posting individual jobs, matching the draft note. Follower count and review/recommendation count **(UNVERIFIED — confirm live).**

**Nextdoor.** Has a Nextdoor business page for Hockley. One snippet cited "**6 Faves**" (single-source, **UNVERIFIED**). Neighbor demand signals present (e.g., requests to "put a stop to the weeds," lawns that have "overtaken" yards) — indicates live local demand in-feed.

**Angi / HomeAdvisor / BBB.** No profile for THIS business was found on Angi, HomeAdvisor, or BBB. (The Angi/HomeAdvisor/BBB "Emerald Lawn & Home Repair, Chris Helms, 9918 Boone Rd, est. 1993" is a **different company** and must not be merged in.)

**Review themes (from the limited visible copy).** Praise/positioning centers on **consistency** ("keep the same person to cut it every time"), **edging/detail and curb appeal for smaller lawns**, and **HOA-compliance** help — all signals that the core service is liked and re-bookable (good for scaling). No recurring complaint themes were extractable (too few visible reviews). Net: **demand and craft are evident; the proof layer (visible reviews) is thin or unverified** — a gap, not a strength.

## 3. Current Funnel Teardown (CORE)

**Website.** No genuine marketing website found for this business. The domains emeraldlawncare.net (GoDaddy, **Western NY**, 716) and emerald-lawncare.com / emeraldlawncareinc.com (NY namesakes) are **NOT this company**. So the draft note "no real marketing site" is **VERIFIED by absence**. The business's web presence = Facebook page + Yelp/Nextdoor directory listings + a Jobber client hub.

**Platform / booking.** They use **Jobber** (clienthub.getjobber.com) (VERIFIED Jobber usage). A Jobber **`/public/work_request/new`** URL surfaced — this is a **work-request / quote-request intake form**, not true self-serve scheduling. So the booking reality is: **Facebook DM / phone / Jobber quote-request form → owner manually quotes → owner schedules.** It is *form-and-phone*, not frictionless online booking. (The exact Jobber form ownership and field set could not be loaded — confirm live.)

**Payment.** Jobber supports online card payment, so card-on-file is *possible*, but whether they actually push customers to pay online vs. cash/Zelle is **UNVERIFIED**. Owner-operators at this stage typically lean cash/Zelle/check.

**SMS / email automation.** Jobber offers automated appointment reminders and follow-ups, but there is **no evidence** they have configured a structured SMS follow-up / review-request / win-back sequence. Given the Gmail-and-Facebook operating style, automation is **almost certainly minimal or off**. **(UNVERIFIED — but low.)**

**Lead-capture quality.** Weak. Inbound depends on a customer (a) finding the Facebook page, (b) sending a DM or calling the cell, or (c) finding the Yelp/Nextdoor "Request a Quote." There is no landing page, no instant-quote, no tracked phone, no pixel-backed retargeting, no review wall to convert browsers.

**SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** There is **no owned landing-page + frictionless-booking front door**. Every lead is routed through a social DM or a manual quote form, then hand-scheduled. Interested prospects who would book at 11pm on a "$X/visit, pick-your-date" page instead bounce, DM and go cold, or click a competitor's instant-quote app. This is precisely the Royal-Pawz pattern (7% → 30.8%): the demand exists, but the path from click to confirmed booking leaks at the handoff.

## 4. Acquisition & Ad Presence

**Google Ads — now?** No evidence of active Google Search Ads. They do not appear to own a site to send paid clicks to. **(UNVERIFIED running ads; assessed as none.)**

**Meta Ad Library.** No active Meta ads were confirmed (Ad Library check is a live-pass item). Given the organic-post style, paid Meta is **assessed unlikely**. **(UNVERIFIED — confirm in Ad Library.)**

**Platform dependency.** Present on **Yelp, Nextdoor, eLocal, YellowPages** as free listings; the category is heavily intermediated by **aggregator apps** (LawnStarter, LawnGuru, GreenPal) that own the "instant quote + book" experience. Emerald is **not** visibly paying Yelp/Angi/Thumbtack — meaning low ad spend but also low managed lead flow.

**Pixels.** No owned site → effectively **no Meta/Google pixel**, so they have **zero retargeting capability** today.

**SEO footprint.** Essentially invisible in organic search for "lawn care Hockley/Cypress." They do not rank for core commercial terms; the SERP is dominated by aggregators and established locals. With no website, there's nothing for Google to rank. This is a greenfield opportunity, not a defended position.

## 5. Owner / Decision-Maker

**Name.** **Not found.** No owner name was confirmable from any primary source (Facebook "About"/page-transparency, Comptroller, and SOS were all gated). The "Chris Helms" name belongs to the unrelated Emerald Lawn & Home Repair and must NOT be attached here. Owner identity **(UNVERIFIED / unknown — confirm via FB Page Transparency live).**

**Reachability.** Direct cell **(713) 904-0575** and personal-style Gmail **EmeraldLawnCare.TX@gmail.com** plus Facebook DM (VERIFIED reachable channels). This is a highly reachable owner-operator — exactly the profile Zapp can talk to directly.

**Personality / signals.** Posts individual job photos, emphasizes detail/edging and HOA compliance, engages on Nextdoor neighbor requests — a hands-on, craft-proud operator who is already doing the marketing manually (and inefficiently). No LinkedIn/personal profile surfaced. Family-owned/woman-owned/bilingual angles: **not confirmed** (unknown).

## 6. Competitive Landscape

In Hockley/Cypress/NW Houston, the prospect competes against three tiers:

1. **Aggregator booking platforms — LawnStarter, LawnGuru, GreenPal.** These own the "instant online quote + book in 2 minutes + app rescheduling + card on file" experience. This is *exactly* the frictionless front door Emerald lacks; they out-convert solo operators on speed-to-book.
2. **Established local brands with sites/franchise marketing — e.g., Lawn Pride of North Houston (national franchise, serves Cypress/Hockley), Copperfield Lawn (since 2012), Southern Scapes Lawn Service (~17 yrs).** These have real websites, service-area SEO pages, and review volume Emerald doesn't have.
3. **Other Facebook/Nextdoor owner-operators** — Emerald's true peer set, where the edge goes to whoever has reviews + a bookable link.

**What competitors have that Emerald lacks:** owned website, instant-quote/online booking, retargeting pixels, and visible review counts. **Where Emerald can realistically win:** it already has the craft, local Nextdoor goodwill, job-photo proof, and Jobber operational backbone. Bolting on a single landing page + frictionless booking + SMS follow-up would let it beat the aggregators on *price/relationship* while matching them on *convenience* — and outrank the solo-operator peer set instantly.

## 7. Vertical Economics & ROI Model

**Ticket / MRR.** Houston-area mowing runs ~**$34–$39 per cut** (avg ~$37). A weekly/bi-weekly recurring client is worth roughly **$145/mo** weekly or ~$1,700–$2,400/yr LTV (VERIFIED ranges from 2026 pricing guides). Mulch/bed jobs are higher one-off tickets ($200–$600+).

**Paid-search economics (lawn/landscape).** Industry CPL ≈ **$40–$120** (avg ~$88), dipping to ~$40–$50 in the spring rush; typically **2–5 leads to close one recurring client** (VERIFIED benchmark ranges).

**Geo caveat (per brief).** Affluent-geo framing (Memorial/Briar Forest/Energy Corridor) does NOT match this Hockley/Cypress operator. ROI should be modeled on **mid-market suburban Cypress/Bridgeland/Towne Lake households** — solid recurring lawns, HOA-driven demand, but lower per-ticket ceilings than Memorial estates.

**Royal-Pawz-style lift, in their dollars (illustrative).** Say Emerald currently converts ~10% of inbound DMs/quote-requests into booked recurring clients and lands ~6 new recurring clients/month organically. A landing-page + frictionless-booking + SMS follow-up system in the Royal-Pawz mold (booking conversion roughly 2–3x) could lift that to ~18–25 booked clients/month.
- **+12–19 net new recurring clients/month** × ~$145/mo ≈ **+$1,700–$2,750 in new MRR added every month**, compounding as recurring revenue.
- Even modestly: doubling the booking rate on existing lead flow is worth **four-figures of new monthly recurring revenue** with no extra ad spend — and a tracked landing page finally makes paid Google Ads viable at a knowable CAC. (Client/conversion baselines are **illustrative/UNVERIFIED**; pricing inputs VERIFIED.)

## 8. Pitch Hook & Fit Summary

**Why them / why now (one line).** A craft-proud, Jobber-equipped owner-operator already generating organic demand on Facebook/Nextdoor — but with **no website, no bookable front door, and unharvested reviews** — is leaking nearly every lead at the manual-quote handoff; a single landing-page + frictionless-booking + SMS system captures revenue they're already earning the right to.

**The lead gap.** No owned landing page; "booking" = Facebook DM / phone / Jobber quote-request form, hand-scheduled; thin/unverified public reviews; zero retargeting; invisible in organic search.

**Recommended Zapp tier.** **Revenue Diagnostic first.** Two unknowns gate a full build: (a) actual revenue scale/owner ambition (is this a side hustle or a growth-minded operator?) and (b) real review/lead volume. A Diagnostic confirms the entity, owner, review counts, and willingness — then graduate to a **Revenue System Build** (landing page → Jobber online booking → Twilio SMS follow-up → Google Ads) if it qualifies.

**Fit score rationale (62).** Strong on the structural fit: genuine owner-operator, thin conversion infra, reachable by cell/DM, recurring-revenue model, already on Jobber (fast integration). Marked **down** for: (1) geography is **Hockley/Cypress, not affluent West Houston** — lower ticket ceilings; (2) unknown revenue scale and owner ambition; (3) unverified entity/owner/review data; (4) lawn care has thinner per-ticket economics and heavier aggregator competition than the dog-grooming proof case. Confidence on "early-stage" is **MODERATE** — it is plausibly early/small, but review and tenure data are unverified, so a hidden-established scenario isn't fully excluded (demotion rule applied as a flag, not a cut).

**Diligence flags.** (1) Heavy namesake contamination — at least 4 unrelated "Emerald Lawn Care/Lawns" entities (TX statewide brand, NW Houston home-repair, Clay NY, Western NY); keep them out of the file. (2) Entity, owner, formation date, and all review counts are UNVERIFIED and must be confirmed live. (3) Confirm the Hockley street address and whether the Jobber form is true booking vs. quote-request.

verification.notes: DISAMBIGUATION IS THE #1 RISK FOR THE PITCH. At least four distinct 'Emerald' lawn companies in TX get conflated by search-engine AI summaries: (1) TARGET = 'Emerald Lawn Care, LLC', Houston, Facebook handle EmeraldLawnCare.TX, runs on Jobber, no owned website, no confirmable Google/Yelp counts; (2) 'Emerald Lawn & Home Repair', Houston — owner CHRIS HELMS, 9918 Boone Rd, started 1994, ~4.7 on Angi/HomeAdvisor, BBB-accredited (DIFFERENT company; do not use its reviews/owner); (3) 'Emerald Lawns', Round Rock/Austin — 473 Yelp reviews, since 2006, statewide brand (DIFFERENT); (4) 'Emerald Lawncare' Burleson / 'Emerald Lawn & Turf' Abilene (DIFFERENT). Every WebSearch AI-summary in this session wrongly merged Chris Helms / 1993-1994 founding / 4.7 rating onto the target — the pitch must NOT repeat that error. Methodology caveat: the Texas Comptroller franchise search is AJAX-only (no static-fetch or URL-param read) and Bizapedia/OpenCorporates are bot-walled, so TX entity formation date stays genuinely unverifiable; do not assert a formation year. The shared browser session was being actively driven by a parallel workflow (tabs hijacked mid-action, keystrokes interleaved into the same form field), so no live interactive Comptroller/Ad-Library result could be trusted this session — confidence MODERATE because the unverifiable items are 'tooling/access blocked' rather than 'evidence of absence'. For the pitch, lead with the verifiable, defensible weaknesses: no owned marketing website, Jobber form/quote-request only (no self-serve booking), and no discoverable Google Business Profile — all confirmed. Avoid stating owner name, formation date, or any review count as fact.

dossier.name: Bella's Neat Maid Services
dossier.vertical: Residential & light-commercial house cleaning / maid service
dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & verification

**Legal/brand name:** Bella's Neat Maid Services (also styled "Bellas Neat Maid Services"). Operating name tied to owner **Martha Pedraza** (UNVERIFIED — Buzzfile + corroborated by multiple customer reviews that name "Martha" performing on-site QA). No confirmed LLC/Inc suffix located.

**Vertical:** House cleaning / maid service — residential plus apartment, office, janitorial and commercial cleaning. Pitches eco-friendly/green, bonded, insured, bilingual (English/Spanish), background-checked staff. (VERIFIED via own site.)

**Service area:** Sprawling, NOT Energy-Corridor-focused. Site lists Houston, Energy Corridor, Galleria, River Oaks, Montrose, Rice Village, Katy, Sugar Land, Missouri City, Stafford, Richmond, Rosenberg, Cypress, Jersey Village, Sharpstown ("Sharptown"). (VERIFIED via site.) The dedicated Energy Corridor page lists **no zip codes** and is templated boilerplate — there is no real geo-targeting or local depth for the affluent West-Houston pocket. (VERIFIED.)

**Operating base / NAP:** Inconsistent and scattered — a material local-SEO problem.
- Homepage shows TWO addresses: service address **2509 McClendon St, Houston, TX 77030** (Medical Center) and "Main Office/Mailing **2606 Skelton Dr, Houston, TX 77067**" (Greenspoint / far North Houston). (VERIFIED via site.)
- Yelp shows **2505 McClendon St**; Angi shows **2437 Southgate Blvd**; Manta shows **77077**; Buzzfile shows owner residential **13022 Thatcher Dr, Houston, TX 77077** (Energy Corridor residential zip). (UNVERIFIED, single-source each.)
- The seed's "office at 2606 Skelton Dr / Energy Corridor" is misleading: 2606 Skelton (77067) is North Houston/Greenspoint, ~20 miles from the Energy Corridor. The only true Energy-Corridor tie is the owner's home zip (77077). (VERIFIED address geography.)

**Phone:** Inconsistent across listings — **(281) 531-0996** primary; also seen (281) 371-6932 (site), (281) 612-6662 (BestProsInTown), (832) 799-8846 (TrustAnalytica). Multiple numbers = fragmented call tracking / NAP. (UNVERIFIED which is live.) Site, email (info@bellasneatmaidservices.com) and phone all appear active. (VERIFIED site live.)

**Texas entity status + formation:** Could NOT confirm in this session. Texas Comptroller franchise-tax and SOS searches are JavaScript-gated and returned no fetchable record; no third-party (opengovus/bizapedia) record surfaced. Formation date UNKNOWN as a primary fact. Best proxy: Yelp states "Established in 2014" and Buzzfile states "~12 years" operating — both point to ~2014, i.e. an **established ~12-year-old business, not early-stage**. (UNVERIFIED, secondary sources, but mutually corroborating.)

**License:** House cleaning requires no Texas occupational license (no TDLR/TDA/TCEQ board applies), so "license status" is n/a. "Bonded and insured" is self-claimed on the site and unverifiable here. (VERIFIED claim exists; bond/insurance itself UNVERIFIED.)

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

This is an **established brand with a real review base**, not a thin footprint — the single most important correction to the seed.

- **Google Business Profile:** ~**28–39 Google reviews**, ~**4.5 stars** (UNVERIFIED exact count — aggregators disagree: ChamberOfCommerce mirrors 28 reviews / 4.5 with a 23-1-1-1-2 distribution; Birdeye aggregates 40 total of which 39 are tagged Google; TrustAnalytica 24 reviews / 4.6). Reviews span 2022–2025, confirming a live, claimed-feeling GBP. (UNVERIFIED count; live check required.)
- **Yelp:** Listed (2505 McClendon St), 13 photos, "Established 2014." Review count not extracted (Yelp blocks fetch); appears low-volume. (UNVERIFIED.)
- **HomeAdvisor / Angi:** Rated ~3.9/5 (lower than Google). (UNVERIFIED count.)
- **Birdeye:** 4.5 / 40 (mostly Google passthrough), profile **unclaimed** by owner. One "review" is obvious spam/lewd content — minor moderation/quality risk on aggregators. (VERIFIED unclaimed.)
- **Facebook (@BellasNeat) + Instagram (@bellasneatmaidservices):** both exist; content walled (login) so follower counts and post cadence UNKNOWN. Active enough to have multiple dated posts indexed. (VERIFIED exist.)
- **Groupon:** runs a "24% off, two hours of cleaning" deal — i.e. they buy acquisition via discounting. (VERIFIED listing exists.)

**Praise themes (proof the service is worth scaling):** thoroughness/attention to detail; punctual, professional, friendly, trustworthy crews; strong recovery service — **owner Martha personally re-visits to fix issues**; multiple multi-year loyal clients ("used them 4–5 years"); reasonable/transparent pricing (calls if a job runs long). High repeat/referral intent. This is a fundamentally good operator with genuine word-of-mouth — exactly the kind of service that rewards a better top-of-funnel.

**Complaint themes (pain points):** **quality inconsistency as they scale** — a loyal 4-yr client documented declining quality, "newer girls on phones," skipped areas; one billing dispute (quoted 2 cleaners, sent 3, charged more) plus a broken-item claim. The recurring thread is **crew-consistency / ops-at-scale**, not demand. Demand is there; throughput quality wobbles.

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

**Website:** bellasneatmaidservices.com — live, multi-page (home, services, what-we-clean, about, contact, ~15 templated service-area pages). Copy is polished/modern-sounding. **Platform not definitively identified** (no footer builder credit exposed; reads like a templated small-business build — WordPress or Duda/Wix class). Service-area pages are near-duplicate boilerplate with no zips, a classic thin-content "doorway page" pattern that Google discounts. (VERIFIED structure; platform UNVERIFIED.)

**Booking:** **Phone-and-form only. There is NO online booking, NO scheduler, NO calendar, NO instant quote.** Every CTA is "CLICK HERE FOR YOUR FREE ESTIMATE" → /contact-us form, or "Make an Appointment 281-531-0996," or an email link. (VERIFIED.)

**Quote flow:** manual — free estimate by phone/email/form, almost certainly a human callback and likely an in-person or text walkthrough to price. High-friction, slow, leaks after-hours leads. (VERIFIED form-only; manual-quote inferred.)

**Payment:** No online payment shown anywhere; no pricing published on the site (third-party aggregator lists ranges like $40–70 per room, $150–250 deep clean, but the business itself publishes nothing). Almost certainly invoice/cash/card-on-site. (VERIFIED no online pay; pricing UNVERIFIED.)

**Follow-up automation:** None detected — no SMS/email nurture, no booking confirmations, no review-request automation evident. Reviews are accumulating organically, not via a request engine. (UNVERIFIED but no evidence of any.)

**Lead-capture quality:** weak — single generic contact form, no qualification fields, no instant response, fragmented phone numbers across listings so call attribution is impossible.

**SINGLE BIGGEST CONVERSION LEAK:** **No instant online booking or instant quote.** A 12-year, 4.5-star, demand-proven cleaner is funneling 100% of interested visitors into a manual "request a free estimate → wait for a callback" loop. Every after-hours, weekend, or impatient high-intent visitor in an affluent West-Houston zip who wants to book a recurring clean tonight has nowhere to convert and bounces to a competitor's "Book in 60 seconds" button. Compounding it: scattered NAP (3+ addresses, 4 phone numbers) splinters the local SEO/GBP authority that should be feeding that funnel.

## 4. Acquisition & ad presence

- **Google Ads:** None detected for their core terms; they do not appear to dominate paid search. (UNVERIFIED — live Ads Transparency Center check needed.)
- **Meta Ad Library:** No active ads surfaced. (UNVERIFIED — live check needed.)
- **Pixels:** Could not confirm Meta/Google pixels (platform not exposed). (UNKNOWN.)
- **Platform dependency:** Present on HomeAdvisor/Angi (rated 3.9), Yelp, Manta, Groupon (active discount deal), Birdeye, plus many directory aggregators. **Groupon discounting is their visible paid-acquisition lever** — margin-eroding, one-and-done buyers, not a repeat-revenue engine.
- **SEO footprint:** Broad but shallow. They rank for their own brand name and appear in directory long-tail, but the service-area pages are thin/templated with no zips — unlikely to rank for competitive money terms like "house cleaning Energy Corridor" against incumbents. The scattered NAP actively suppresses local-pack performance. They are **organically present but not winning paid or competitive organic search.**

## 5. Owner / decision-maker

- **Owner / principal:** **Martha Pedraza** (UNVERIFIED — Buzzfile; strongly corroborated by reviews repeatedly naming "Martha" doing personal on-site QA). Woman-owned, family-owned, bilingual (Spanish/English) — three strong relatability angles for a Zapp pitch. (VERIFIED woman/family/bilingual claims on site.)
- **Management contact:** A review response is signed **"Maria Leija, Bella's Neat Maid Services Management"** — a likely second decision-maker / office manager and a useful secondary contact. (VERIFIED via review response.)
- **Direct contact:** (281) 531-0996; info@bellasneatmaidservices.com. Owner residential/registered address likely 13022 Thatcher Dr, 77077 (Energy Corridor). (UNVERIFIED.)
- **LinkedIn / personal web presence:** none found this session.
- **Personality signals (strong, positive):** Martha personally re-cleans and meets unhappy clients; management writes thoughtful, accountable review responses (offered a free full re-clean to a dissatisfied loyal client). This is a **hands-on, service-proud, accountable owner** — high coachability, cares about quality and reputation. Ideal psychographic for a done-for-you growth system; she clearly wants to delight customers but is bottlenecked by manual ops and a manual funnel.

## 6. Competitive landscape

West-Houston / Energy-Corridor maid competitors that out-equip Bella's on the funnel:
1. **The Maids (Houston)** — national brand, online instant-quote flow, "10% off first clean," heavy SEO/brand trust.
2. **Naturalcare Cleaning Service** — ~100 reviews / 4.7, eco-positioning that directly overlaps Bella's "green" angle but with far more review volume and a polished booking path.
3. **Maid Brigade / Molly Maid of NW Houston** — 99–100 reviews / 4.8, franchise SEO muscle, online scheduling.
4. **Cleanzen / Detail Cleaning / Maids and Moore** — modern "book online in 60 seconds," published flat pricing (e.g. "$89"), 800+ reviews in one case.

**What they have that Bella's lacks:** instant online booking, published/transparent pricing, far higher review counts, clean single-NAP local SEO, and visible paid/optimized acquisition. **Where Bella's can realistically win:** it already has the hardest-to-fake asset — a 12-year track record, 4.5 stars, genuine repeat clients, a hands-on bilingual owner, and the affluent Energy-Corridor/Memorial/Briar-Forest residential market on its doorstep (owner lives in 77077). Bella's loses purely on **funnel and discoverability mechanics**, not on service quality — precisely the gap Zapp closes.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

**Ticket sizes (West Houston):** standard recurring clean ~$120–180; deep clean ~$150–250; move-out/post-construction ~$180–350 (aggregator ranges, UNVERIFIED). The money is in **recurring bi-weekly/weekly plans** — high LTV, the ideal product for an automated booking + SMS-retention system.

**Paid-search economics (Houston maid vertical):** "house cleaning" CPCs typically run ~$8–18; with a thin form-only funnel, lead→booked conversion is poor, so blended CAC is high. With an optimized LP + instant booking + SMS follow-up (the Royal Pawz playbook), the same ad and organic clicks convert far better, dropping effective CAC and lifting ROAS.

**Seasonality:** spring-cleaning (Mar–May) and holiday (Nov–Dec) peaks; move-season (summer) drives move-out cleans — strong, plannable demand swings to capture with always-on booking.

**Royal-Pawz-style ROI math (illustrative):** Buzzfile estimates ~$242K/yr ≈ **~$20K/mo**. If, like Royal Pawz, frictionless online booking + SMS follow-up lifts the visitor→booked-job rate from a typical ~7% to even ~20% (a far smaller jump than Royal Pawz's 7%→30.8%), that's roughly a **2.8x** lift on conversion of existing demand. On a $20K base, recapturing even a third of currently-leaked high-intent traffic into recurring plans is plausibly **+$5K–10K/mo** in incremental, mostly-recurring revenue — at a per-clean LTV that compounds. The proof of demand already exists in the reviews; the leak is purely at the booking step. (All figures UNVERIFIED / illustrative — base revenue is a third-party estimate.)

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

**Why-them-why-now (one line):** A 12-year, 4.5-star, bilingual woman-owned Houston cleaner with proven repeat demand is running 100% of its interest through a manual "request a free estimate and wait for a callback" form — no online booking, no scheduler, no SMS follow-up, and a scattered 3-address/4-phone NAP — while every modern competitor converts the same visitor with a 60-second "Book Now."

**Lead gap:** zero instant-booking / instant-quote capability + fragmented NAP suppressing the local SEO that should feed it; acquisition currently leans on margin-killing Groupon discounting instead of an owned, automated funnel.

**Recommended Zapp tier:** **Revenue System Build.** Demand and reputation are already proven (the diagnostic question — "is the service good enough to scale?" — is answered yes by 4.5 stars and multi-year loyal clients). The constraint is execution infrastructure: build the optimized LP + online booking + Twilio SMS follow-up, consolidate NAP/GBP, and layer tight Energy-Corridor/Memorial/Briar-Forest geo-targeted Google Ads. A short Diagnostic could precede it to baseline current conversion, but Build is the fit.

**Fit score rationale (62):** Strong on owner profile (hands-on, accountable, bilingual, woman-owned), affluent West-Houston geo proximity, recurring high-LTV product, thin-funnel opportunity, and demonstrated coachability — all bullseye for Zapp. **Demotion applied:** the seed's "small/early-stage, thin web footprint" is wrong — this is a ~12-year, ~$242K, ~12-employee, 28–39-review established business, so the easy-greenfield upside is smaller and the real challenge shifts to ops-consistency-at-scale (a documented complaint) and NAP cleanup. Mid-band score: a genuinely good fit on funnel-gap and psychographics, capped by maturity and by ROI figures resting on third-party estimates.

**Diligence flags:** (1) Confirm Texas entity status/formation (Comptroller + SOS) — unverified this session. (2) Verify exact live Google review count and GBP claim status. (3) Resolve which address/phone is canonical before any ad spend — NAP must be unified first. (4) Quality-consistency complaints suggest fulfillment capacity should be confirmed before pouring on demand. (5) Owner name (Pedraza) is single-source — confirm before personalizing outreach.

dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & verification

- **Legal/brand name:** Katy Fence Company (VERIFIED as the operating brand; legal entity name — likely an LLC — NOT confirmed). Website **katyfenceco.com is live** (VERIFIED) and phone **(832) 903-1380** is the published, owner-answered line (VERIFIED across site + multiple listings).
- **Vertical:** Fencing & gate installation — wood/cedar privacy, custom iron, driveway gates, chain-link, commercial, fence replacement, repairs, and staining. Described as a fencing-only shop "365 days a year." (VERIFIED from site.)
- **Service area:** Headline coverage is Katy, Cinco Ranch, Cypress, Fulshear, Rosenberg, Sugar Land, Brookshire and "Houston (Memorial)" (VERIFIED from service-locations page). The site lists business ZIP **77449** (Katy). The seed's specific affluent ZIPs (77079/77024/77094 — Energy Corridor/Memorial/Bunker Hill) are NOT explicitly named on the site; "Memorial / West Houston" is named generically (UNVERIFIED that they actively target those exact zips). Nextdoor activity confirms real jobs in Fulshear, Houston, and Katy neighborhoods.
- **Texas entity status + formation:** Site and The Katy News state **founded January 2019** (UNVERIFIED — promotional sources only). BBB file opened **6/21/2021** (UNVERIFIED). I could **NOT confirm the Texas Comptroller franchise-tax account or SOS entity record** this session — those searches are form-gated and not web-indexable, so legal entity name and "right to transact business" status remain **UNKNOWN**. Do not assert the 2019 date as fact.
- **License + status:** Fencing has **no Texas state contractor license** (n/a — normal for this trade; no TDLR/TDA/TCEQ requirement). The footer cites **"Contractor Registration License CON-004384-2024,"** which is a **City of Katy local contractor registration** (the "-2024" suffix implies a 2024 issue/renewal). Status UNVERIFIED — confirm via City of Katy lookup.
- **Active confirmation:** Clearly **active and operating** — live site, current copyright (© 2026), recent Nextdoor recommendations, and post-Hurricane-Beryl (July 2024) service mentions. This is an operating, demand-generating business, not a dormant shell.

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

- **Google:** Multiple independent secondary sources (The Katy News Jan 2026 feature, PropJump aggregator, a competitor comparison page, and the company's own Facebook) consistently cite **200+ to 220+ five-star Google reviews** (UNVERIFIED — exact live count and the displayed star rating were NOT directly read from Google this session; flagged for live check). Even discounted, the convergence of sources strongly indicates a **large, highly favorable Google footprint** — this is a high-reputation incumbent, not an early-stage operator.
- **Nextdoor:** ~**23 recommendations** and **"Neighborhood Favorite" in 3 neighborhoods** (Polo Ranch/Fulshear, Timberline/Houston, Bridgewater/Katy) (UNVERIFIED, single fetch). Nextdoor is clearly a meaningful demand channel for them.
- **BBB:** Profile exists; **C+ rating, not accredited, 1 complaint that went unanswered** (UNVERIFIED). The unanswered complaint is the one reputational blemish and a small operational-responsiveness flag.
- **Yelp / Facebook / Angi:** A Yelp listing tied to this phone exists (slug "katy-fence-katy"); exact Yelp count not captured (note: a separate generic "Katy Fence Company" Yelp listing with a different phone, 832-402-7525, is a DIFFERENT business — do not conflate). Facebook page is active (follower/rating behind login wall, not captured). No confirmed Angi profile for this specific company.
- **Review themes — praise (proof the service is worth scaling):** transparent/consistent pricing, courteous crews, daily jobsite cleanup, **Oscar personally walks the finished fence with the customer**, fast turnaround (some jobs in one day), strong communication, showed up promptly after Hurricane Beryl when others didn't, good HOA handling. **Complaints:** essentially none surfaced publicly aside from the single unanswered BBB complaint. The praise profile (personal owner touch + reliability) is exactly the kind of service quality that rewards more lead volume — but they are already capturing a lot of it.

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

- **Website:** Professional **WordPress** site, **built and maintained by Contractor Growth Network** (a fencing/home-services marketing agency — named in the footer). This is a meaningful signal: **they already have a marketing partner**, so a Zapp pitch competes with an incumbent agency rather than filling a vacuum. Site is multi-page, polished, mobile-reasonable, with service, about, portfolio, featured-projects, and contact pages. Not a templated UENI/GoDaddy one-pager.
- **Booking:** **Form-only + phone.** The contact form is actually well-designed for a fence shop — it captures first/last name, email, phone, address, service type (dropdown), project description, **preferred callback window**, referral source, timeline, and **photo upload**. But there is **NO online scheduling/calendar, NO instant quote, NO text-to-quote, NO chat widget.** Every lead routes to a manual promise: **"Oscar with Katy Fence Company will contact you directly."**
- **Payment:** Deposit after approved site visit, balance on completion (VERIFIED from site language). No online payment/financing offered (UNVERIFIED beyond site copy).
- **Follow-up automation:** **None detected.** No SMS opt-in language, no evident auto-reply, no email nurture. No Meta/Google pixels visible in the homepage source — suggesting **weak/absent conversion tracking and no retargeting.**
- **SINGLE biggest conversion leak:** The **manual, owner-dependent lead handoff with zero instant response and no self-serve scheduling.** A homeowner who fills the form (or 5 competing forms) waits for Oscar to call back. There's no instant SMS auto-reply to claim the lead, no online "pick your site-visit slot," and no automated nudge if the callback slips — so speed-to-lead and after-hours leads leak to faster competitors. This is the textbook Royal-Pawz gap (frictionless booking + Twilio SMS follow-up), and it's real here. The caveat: with 200+ reviews their **inbound demand is already strong**, so the leak is a margin/efficiency improvement on a healthy funnel, not a survival fix.

## 4. Acquisition & ad presence

- **Google Ads / LSA:** **None detected.** No evidence of Local Services Ads ("Google Guaranteed"/"Google Screened") or sponsored search listings for their terms. (LIVE CHECK — confirm on a clean Google session.)
- **Meta ads:** **No active ads confirmed.** The Meta Ad Library page errored/login-walled this session, so this is UNVERIFIED — flagged for a live check, but no indirect evidence of paid social either.
- **Tracking:** **No Meta Pixel or Google Analytics/Ads tag visible** in the fetched homepage source — consistent with a primarily organic operation and weak conversion measurement.
- **Platform dependency:** Appears to lean on **organic SEO (agency-managed), Nextdoor, Google Business Profile reviews, and word-of-mouth/referral** (their form even lists "Door Hanger, Yard Sign, Truck Wrap, Referral" as sources — classic local trade mix). Not visibly dependent on Yelp Ads / Angi / Thumbtack / HomeAdvisor.
- **SEO footprint:** They surface across many fence-related queries and aggregators for Katy/Cypress and rank as a recognized name alongside Maverick and Hill Fence — i.e., they are **already reasonably visible**, not invisible. This further lowers the "we'll turn the lights on for you" Zapp angle and pushes toward a conversion/automation angle.

## 5. Owner / decision-maker

- **Owner:** **Oscar Gutierrez** (founder/owner) — VERIFIED. Trades since ~2012 (learned under his father, a general contractor); faith-based, family-owned positioning. His **brother Carlos** reportedly leads the field crew (UNVERIFIED — single source; the About page itself doesn't name Carlos).
- **Personality / contact signals:** **Highly owner-operated** — Oscar personally answers (832) 903-1380, personally calls leads back, and personally walks the finished fence with customers. He engages on Nextdoor (e.g., answered a neighbor's "puppy panel" question). This is a reachable, hands-on owner-operator — a genuine plus for Zapp's single-operator pitch. Tagline "Texas Strong Fences By Strong Men With Strong Faith"; donates 10% of net profit to three named Christian nonprofits.
- **Angles:** Family-owned + faith-driven + community (Nextdoor/Beryl) story is strong. **Bilingual/Spanish angle not confirmed** on-site despite Hispanic ownership (don't assume). No personal LinkedIn confirmed this session.

## 6. Competitive landscape

- **Maverick Fence of Katy** (Keith) — **~4.9 stars, 230–250+ Google reviews, 15+ years, ~20 employees.** The dominant Katy/Cinco Ranch incumbent. Strong review moat; still **free-estimate/phone-and-form based** (no obvious online scheduling), so the booking-automation edge is available against them too.
- **Hill Fence** (katyfence.org) — veteran/family-owned, **established 2005**, broad material range, free quotes; long-tenured SEO presence.
- **Katy Fence Repair** (since ~2010), plus national franchises pushing into the market (**Superior Fence & Rail of West Houston**) and SEO aggressors (Aber, Houston Fence, 76 Fence).
- **What rivals have that this prospect lacks:** Maverick has a deeper/longer review moat and more crews. The national franchises (Superior Fence & Rail) bring **professionalized intake and sometimes online quote tooling**. **What NO local rival visibly has — and where Katy Fence Company can win — is true online self-scheduling + instant SMS speed-to-lead.** Whoever responds in 60 seconds and lets a homeowner book a site visit online beats the "we'll call you back" pack. That is the realistic wedge.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

- **Ticket size:** Houston-area wood/privacy fence runs **~$23–$45 per linear foot**; a typical residential job (~150 ft) lands **~$3,600–$6,300**, with cedar/iron and gates pushing higher. Call it a **conservative ~$4,500 average installed ticket** for residential.
- **Volume:** Site claims **600+ projects/year** (UNVERIFIED). Even at a conservative blend, that implies a **multi-million-dollar topline** — reinforcing that this is an established operator.
- **CAC / ROAS (West Houston fencing, paid search):** Fence keywords are competitive; expect **~$8–$20+ per click** and meaningful cost-per-qualified-lead, but with a $4,500 ticket and ~30–45% gross margin, even a few-hundred-dollar CAC yields strong ROAS. Seasonality: peak spring–early fall, **storm-driven surges** (post-hurricane fence replacement is a major demand spike in Houston).
- **Royal-Pawz-style conversion math:** Suppose they currently get **~120 form/call leads a month** and convert ~25% to booked jobs = **~30 jobs**. Lifting booked-conversion via instant SMS + online scheduling from 25% to even **40%** (a Royal-Pawz-scale lift) = **~48 jobs** — **+18 jobs/month**. At a $4,500 ticket and ~35% gross margin (~$1,575 gross/job), that's **~$28k incremental monthly profit / ~$340k/year** from the *existing* lead flow, before any new ad spend. The leverage here is **converting the demand they already earn**, not buying more traffic. (All figures illustrative — anchor to their real lead count in the Diagnostic.)

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

- **Why-them-why-now:** A faith-driven, owner-operated shop that has *already won the hard part* — 200+ five-star reviews and Nextdoor "Neighborhood Favorite" status — but is still **leaking booked jobs through a manual "Oscar will call you back" funnel** with no instant response and no online scheduling. The demand is there; the conversion infrastructure isn't.
- **Lead gap:** No online booking, no instant SMS speed-to-lead, no follow-up automation, no visible conversion tracking/retargeting.
- **Recommended tier:** **Revenue Diagnostic** (not a full Build, at least initially). Rationale: they already have (a) a strong brand/reviews, (b) a competent multi-page site, and critically (c) **an existing marketing agency, Contractor Growth Network.** A Diagnostic lets Zapp quantify their actual lead-to-booked conversion and prove the speed-to-lead/scheduling gap in their own dollars without immediately colliding with the incumbent agency. If the Diagnostic shows a big conversion leak and the agency isn't owning booking automation, upsell to a focused **booking + Twilio SMS Build** (Zapp's exact wheelhouse) layered on top of, not replacing, their site.
- **Fit score rationale (42/100):** **DEMOTED.** The seed framed this as an early-stage, Nextdoor-driven, invisible-on-Google operator — the research shows the **opposite**: an established, high-review incumbent with an existing marketing partner and decent SEO. Zapp's strongest selling motion (turn on acquisition for someone with nothing) doesn't apply; the realistic angle is narrower (conversion/automation overlay). Positives keeping it above ~35: a **genuine, real conversion leak**, a **reachable hands-on owner-operator**, high-ticket recurring-ish work, and an affluent West-Houston catchment. Negatives capping it: established brand + strong inbound demand (less urgency), incumbent agency (channel conflict / "who owns the site" friction), and unverified entity/exact-review data.
- **Diligence flags:** (1) Exact Google review count/rating unconfirmed — if it's truly 200+, hold the low fit score; (2) **existing agency relationship (Contractor Growth Network)** must be navigated; (3) Texas entity status & formation date unverified; (4) one unanswered BBB complaint (minor responsiveness flag); (5) ad presence (Meta/Google) confirmed only by absence-of-evidence, needs a live check.

dossier.dossierMarkdown:
## 1. Identity & verification

- **Brand / DBA:** Southern Roots Fencing (a.k.a. "Southern Roots Fence Company," "Southern Roots Fencing and Supply"). **Legal entity:** *Southern Roots Fencing and Supply LLC*, Katy, TX — confirmed to exist via Bizapedia listing (VERIFIED entity exists; **formation date UNVERIFIED** — Bizapedia/OpenCorporates captcha-blocked this session, and the Comptroller name search could not be completed via URL).
- **Vertical:** Fencing — residential + commercial fence installation, repair/maintenance, custom gates & operators, wood-stain, plus a retail fence-supply arm. Differentiator they market: "stick-built" custom fences, **not** pre-fab panels. (VERIFIED from site.)
- **Service area:** Headquartered at **5217 Meadowlark Ln, Katy, TX 77493** (VERIFIED, consistent across site/Thumbtack/aggregators). Marketed coverage is far wider than the seed's "Cypress/Katy": Katy, Cypress, Houston, Sugar Land, Fulshear, Richmond, Rosenberg, Tomball, Pearland, Pasadena, The Woodlands, Brookshire, Simonton, Wallis, Sealy — **plus** Brenham, Bryan, Navasota, Anderson, Wallis (a Brazos Valley cluster, suggesting a second market or heavy programmatic-SEO city pages). (VERIFIED from site footer/landing pages.)
- **Phone:** (832) 604-2563 (VERIFIED, consistent everywhere). **Owner email:** cameron@txsouthernroots.com (UNVERIFIED — single search snapshot; note the *txsouthernroots.com* domain differs from the main site).
- **License:** **Texas has no statewide fence-contractor license** — fencing is not a TDLR/TDA/TCEQ-regulated trade. The site's "licensed and insured" is a generic, self-asserted claim, not a verifiable state credential (VERIFIED that no such license exists; their insurance is UNVERIFIED).
- **Active confirmation:** Site is live, multi-page, professionally built; phone and address are consistent across third-party listings. Business is clearly operating. (VERIFIED.)

## 2. Reputation & demand proof

- **Google reviews:** The seed's **"20+ five-star reviews (low count)" is wrong and is stale website boilerplate.** The Trustindex widget on their domain pulls **100 Google reviews at 5.0** (99×5★, 1×4★); a separate search snapshot showed **88** — both far above "20+." The site's own Cypress page displays a **"5.0 / 215 reviews"** aggregate badge. **Exact live Google Business Profile count is UNVERIFIED** and must be read in-map (the 88/100/215 spread needs reconciliation), but the floor is ~90+ Google reviews — an **established, high-trust brand.** (Trustindex VERIFIED; exact GBP number UNVERIFIED.)
- **Other platforms:** Thumbtack 5.0 / **43 reviews**, 1 hire, profile present but **"not actively bidding"** (VERIFIED). ProvenExpert profile is live but currently shows **"Not reviewed"/0** and was only created 04/2026 — earlier "43 on ProvenExpert" snippets are unreliable (UNVERIFIED). LocallyFind aggregator cites "66 reviews." **No BBB profile** found for the Katy fencing entity. Yelp, Nextdoor, and Instagram presence **not confirmed** this session.
- **Review themes — top praise (proof it's worth scaling):** quality/precision of stick-built custom work; clean job sites; strong communication; owner **Cameron** personally named as helpful; fast turnaround (jobs done in ~1.5 days). **Recurring complaints:** none surfaced — rating is near-perfect 5.0 across sources, which itself signals genuine demand and a product worth pouring more leads into. (VERIFIED themes; absence of complaints UNVERIFIED as comprehensive.)

## 3. Current funnel teardown (THE CORE)

- **Website:** **WordPress** (wp-content artifacts) — professionally designed, not a templated UENI/GoDaddy throwaway. Heavy programmatic city/service landing-page footprint (dozens of "[City] Fence Company" pages). (VERIFIED.)
- **Booking:** **Form-only.** Primary CTA is "Get A Quick Quote," routing to a **4-step request form** (fence specs → property details → contact info + "best time to call" → notes). Secondary CTA is "Give us a call." **There is NO online scheduling/calendar, NO instant quote, NO appointment booking.** (VERIFIED.)
- **Payment:** Offline. Thumbtack lists cash, check, credit card, Venmo, Zelle; financing is mentioned on-site "ask a rep." **No online checkout/deposit.** (VERIFIED.)
- **Follow-up automation:** **None detected.** No evidence of post-submit auto-confirmation, SMS, or email nurture. The form's "best time to call" field implies a **manual, human-callback** process — exactly the slow-response leak Zapp closes. (UNVERIFIED that none exists, but no signal of any.)
- **Lead-capture quality:** The form captures good structured data (specs, address, contact, timing) — but it's long (4 steps = friction on mobile) and ends in a manual queue. **SINGLE BIGGEST CONVERSION LEAK:** a multi-step form with **no instant response and no automated follow-up**, sitting on top of strong organic + 90+ five-star demand. Leads that fill it out wait on a human callback; mobile users likely abandon mid-form. This is the Royal Pawz pattern (7% → 30.8%) almost exactly — strong top-of-funnel, leaky conversion layer.

## 4. Acquisition & ad presence

- **Google Ads:** **None clearly detected** (UNVERIFIED — needs live SERP check). They rely on **organic SEO**, ranking ~**position 7** on page one for "fence installation Katy TX" — present but mid-pack, not dominating. Their dozens of city pages are a programmatic-SEO play. (VERIFIED organic presence; rank approximate.)
- **Meta ads:** Meta Ad Library fetch was blocked this session — **status UNVERIFIED**; no evidence found that they are currently running Meta ads.
- **Platform dependency:** Low — Thumbtack profile exists but is **"not actively bidding"**; no Angi/HomeAdvisor/Yelp-Ads dependency surfaced. They are **not** locked into a lead-gen marketplace, which means Zapp-owned acquisition is greenfield. (VERIFIED low dependency.)
- **Pixels/tracking:** Not confirmed — needs source inspection. If GA4/Meta Pixel/Google Ads tags are absent, they currently **cannot measure or retarget** conversions (a Diagnostic talking point). (UNVERIFIED.)
- **SEO footprint:** Solid for an SMB — page-one organic, large indexed city-page footprint, Trustindex review widget for social proof. They are **visible**, which makes the conversion fix (not traffic) the highest-leverage move.

## 5. Owner / decision-maker

- **Owner/Founder: Cameron Roesler** (VERIFIED across multiple sources). Brand voice is personal and craftsmanship-driven: *"The lessons I learned in my grandfather's garage are the foundation I've built my company upon… Perfection isn't the goal, perfection is the mission,"* and "my name is on every fence." This is a **proud owner-operator who personally signs off on quality** — a strong fit for a founder-to-founder Zapp pitch.
- **Contact:** Direct email cameron@txsouthernroots.com (UNVERIFIED) and main line (832) 604-2563. Reviewers name "Cameron" directly, indicating he's hands-on in the sales/customer relationship — meaning a slow manual lead process is *his* time being consumed.
- **Team:** Thumbtack self-reports **8 employees** and **~9 years in business** — **a real, staffed company, not a solo early-stage startup** (UNVERIFIED but credible). **LinkedIn** profile not located this session.
- **Angle:** Founder-led, quality-obsessed, "Southern roots" family/craft narrative — receptive to a "you've built the reputation, now stop leaking the leads" message.

## 6. Competitive landscape (Katy / Cypress / West Houston fencing)

- **Superior Fence & Rail (West Houston/Cypress):** national franchise, **30,000+ reviews** system-wide, polished funnel, almost certainly running paid search. Out-resources Southern Roots on volume and ad spend.
- **Hill Fence (Katy):** veteran/family-owned since 2005, **~113 reviews (4.6)** on Birdeye, Nextdoor + BBB presence. Comparable local incumbent with a longer track record but a *lower* star rating than Southern Roots.
- **Katy Fence Company (Oscar Gutierrez):** ~600 projects/yr, decade-plus, contact-form-only funnel (same leak as Southern Roots).
- **Custer Fence (Cypress):** "40+ five-star," smaller review base than Southern Roots.
- **Where Southern Roots can win:** It already has a **best-in-class 5.0 rating on ~90+ Google reviews** (higher rating than Hill, more reviews than Custer) and page-one organic — but **none** of these locals pair that with **instant-response online booking + automated SMS follow-up.** Whoever adds a frictionless conversion layer first converts the same traffic at a far higher rate. That's the wedge.

## 7. Vertical economics & ROI model

- **Ticket size:** Residential wood/privacy fence installs in West Houston typically run **~$3,500–$9,000+** per job (custom stick-built and Trex/metal trend higher); commercial and ranch jobs run larger. Use a **conservative ~$5,000 average installed job.**
- **Paid-search economics:** Fencing CPCs in metro Houston are roughly **$8–$20/click**; cost-per-lead commonly **$60–$150**, and with a ~$5k ticket and healthy margins a tuned Google Ads → fast-response funnel can clear **4–6x ROAS** (in range of the Royal Pawz 5x proof). (Industry-typical, UNVERIFIED for this specific account.)
- **Seasonality:** Spring/summer + post-storm peaks; West Houston demand spikes after wind/freeze events. A booking + SMS system captures storm-surge leads that otherwise overflow a manual callback queue.
- **Royal-Pawz-style lift math (illustrative):** Say they get **100 quote-form leads/month** and currently close ~**10%** = **10 jobs**. At a $5,000 ticket that's **$50,000/mo**. A conversion lift on the *same* leads — even a modest move from 10% → 18% via instant response + automated follow-up (Royal Pawz roughly *quadrupled* its booking rate, so this is conservative) = **18 jobs = $90,000/mo**, i.e. **~+$40,000/month (~+$480k/yr)** with **zero extra ad spend**. Layer a managed Google Ads top-of-funnel on top and the upside compounds. For a high-ticket trade like fencing, even a few extra closed jobs per month dwarfs Zapp's fee — the math is unusually favorable.

## 8. Pitch hook & fit summary

- **Why them, why now:** They've already done the hard part — ~90+ five-star Google reviews, a 5.0 rating, page-one organic, a hands-on owner who guards quality. The bottleneck isn't reputation or traffic; it's a **4-step form with no instant response and no follow-up automation** quietly leaking high-ticket leads. That's precisely the gap Zapp closed for Royal Pawz (7% → 30.8%).
- **The lead gap:** No online scheduling, no auto-reply, no SMS/email nurture, manual human callback, no confirmed conversion tracking.
- **Recommended tier: Revenue Diagnostic first.** This is an established 8-person, ~9-year company, so the entry is a paid diagnostic that quantifies the real form-abandon + slow-response leak and time-to-first-contact, then upsells to a System Build (booking + Twilio SMS + landing page + managed Ads). A Build can follow once the diagnostic proves the dollar leak.
- **Fit score 58 / MODERATE confidence, with a demotion applied.** The seed framed this as an "early-stage, 20+ review, family local" lead — **it is not.** It's a mature, well-reviewed, multi-city brand with staff and SEO. Per the demotion rule, fit drops from the high-fit "invisible early operator" band: they may already have internal process, the owner is busy/successful, and they have wider geography than a single-operator system assumes. **It stays a real prospect** because the conversion-layer gap is genuine, the trade is high-ticket, the owner is reachable and founder-led, and they're not locked into a lead-gen marketplace.
- **Diligence flags:** (1) Reconcile the 88 vs 100 vs 215 review numbers live; (2) confirm formation date / true age (Thumbtack "9 years" unverified); (3) confirm whether any CRM/automation already exists before pitching "you have none"; (4) the Brazos-Valley city cluster suggests possible second market / heavier ops than a Katy-only read implies; (5) affluent-geo ROI framing should target **Memorial / Briar Forest / Energy Corridor** residential fence replacements, not the lower-income Westchase pocket.

- **Website / platform:** texmexjunk.com on **GoDaddy Website Builder** (templated; "Powered by GoDaddy" footer), copyright **2025**, reCAPTCHA on form.
